     1	# CLAUDE.md — ZINC-FUSION-V16
     2	
     3	**All project rules, hard rules, tech stack, phases, and architecture decisions live in
     4	[`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md).** Read it before doing anything. This file contains only
     5	Claude Code-specific setup instructions.
     6	
     7	---
     8	
     9	## First Session Setup — Claude Code Only
    10	
    11	### 1. Install Claude Code Plugins
    12	
    13	Install in this order — process skills first, then domain skills:
    14	
    15	```bash
    16	# TIER 1: Process Skills (install these FIRST — they govern HOW you work)
    17	/install-plugin superpowers
    18	# Gives you: brainstorming, TDD, debugging, code review, writing-plans, verification
    19	
    20	# TIER 2: Code Quality (install before writing any code)
    21	/install-plugin pr-review-toolkit
    22	/install-plugin code-simplifier
    23	/install-plugin commit-commands
    24	
    25	# TIER 3: Stack-Specific (install when you start building)
    26	/install-plugin vercel          # Deployment, logs, env management
    27	/install-plugin frontend-design # UI components, design system
    28	
    29	# TIER 4: Optional but Useful
    30	/install-plugin playwright      # Browser testing (useful for ProFarmer scraper testing)
    31	```
    32	
    33	**Why this order matters:** Superpowers enforces brainstorming before coding, TDD before implementation, and verification before claiming done. Without it, agents tend to cowboy — especially on a fresh repo where there's no existing code to constrain them.
    34	
    35	### 2. Configure MCP Servers
    36	
    37	These MCP servers should be active for this workspace:
    38	
    39	| Server                      | Purpose                                                      | Required? |
    40	| --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | --------- |
    41	| `memory`                    | Knowledge graph — persist decisions across sessions          | **Yes**   |
    42	| `sequentialthinking`        | Structured problem-solving for multi-step tasks              | **Yes**   |
    43	| `context7`                  | Up-to-date library docs (Supabase, Next.js, shadcn)          | **Yes**   |
    44	| `supabase`                  | Direct Supabase management (migrations, SQL, edge functions) | **Yes**   |
    45	| `puppeteer` or `playwright` | Browser automation (ProFarmer testing)                       | Later     |
    46	
    47	**Memory API Contract:** Use graph API tools (`search_nodes`, `create_entities`, `add_observations`, `read_graph`). NOT simple-memory tools (`search_memory`, `list_memories`). If the wrong tools appear, fix the MCP config before proceeding.
    48	
    49	### 3. Read The Migration Plan
    50	
    51	Before writing ANY code, read the full migration plan:
    52	
    53	```
    54	docs/plans/2026-03-17-v16-migration-plan.md
    55	```
    56	
    57	Pay special attention to Sections 4, 5, 10, and 11.
    58	
    59	### 4. Understand the Legacy Baseline Reference
    60	
    61	legacy baseline lives at a separate path (likely `/Volumes/Satechi Hub/ZINC-FUSION-legacy baseline/` or wherever the user has it). It is a **reference library**, not a source of code. You study it for:
    62	
    63	- What the chart looks like and how it behaves
    64	- What data contracts the API routes serve
    65	- What the landing page design looks like
    66	- What the Vegas Intel page contains
    67	- How specialists generate signals
    68	
    69	You do NOT:
    70	
    71	- Copy files from legacy baseline
    72	- Import legacy baseline modules
    73	- Reuse legacy baseline's Prisma migrations
    74	- Carry over legacy baseline's `.env` files
    75	- Port legacy baseline's Inngest functions
     1	# AGENTS.md — ZINC-FUSION-V16
     2	
     3	## What This Project Is
     4	
     5	Commodity procurement forecasting system for ZL (soybean oil futures). Clean-room rebuild of legacy baseline on Supabase — **no code transferred, everything written from scratch.**
     6	
     7	**Client:** US Oil Solutions (Las Vegas)
     8	
     9	- **Chris** (owner) BUYS raw soybean oil by the trainload for 100+ restaurant kitchens (Caesars, Boyd, Resorts World)
    10	- **Kevin** (sales director) uses Vegas Intel to pitch restaurants and schedule service around events
    11	- Chris is a BUYER. ZL going UP = bad for his costs. Strategy language (ACCUMULATE/WAIT) reflects this.
    12	
    13	**The migration plan is your bible:** [`docs/plans/2026-03-17-v16-migration-plan.md`](docs/plans/2026-03-17-v16-migration-plan.md) — 1,235 lines, 14 sections, every table/route/job/phase defined.
    14	
    15	---
    16	
    17	## Tech Stack
    18	
    19	| Layer                  | Technology                                              |
    20	| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
    21	| **Database**           | Supabase Postgres — cloud only, no local Supabase       |
    22	| **Schema mgmt**        | Supabase CLI migrations (SQL-first, `db push` to cloud) |
    23	| **Frontend**           | Next.js 14+ on Vercel (frontend hosting ONLY)           |
    24	| **UI System**          | shadcn/ui + Radix primitives + Tailwind CSS             |
    25	| **Data ingestion**     | pg_cron + `http` extension (inside Postgres, $0 cost)   |
    26	| **DB client (TS)**     | Supabase JS client (reads only)                         |
    27	| **DB client (Python)** | psycopg2 to cloud Supabase Postgres; local PostgreSQL only for AG training source |
    28	| **ML**                 | AutoGluon (CPU-only), custom specialist models          |
    29	| **Auth**               | Supabase Auth                                           |
    30	| **API secrets**        | Supabase Vault (accessed via `current_setting()`)       |
    31	| **Package mgr**        | npm (frontend), uv (Python)                             |
    32	| **Env mgmt**           | Vercel <> Supabase integration, `vercel env pull`       |
    33	
    34	---
    35	
    36	## Hard Rules
    37	
    38	### Architecture Rules
    39	
    40	1. **11 specialists — NEVER say 10.** The Big-11: crush, china, fx, fed, tariff, energy, biofuel, palm, volatility, substitutes, trump_effect.
    41	2. **Target = future PRICE LEVEL** (`close.shift(-horizon)`), columns named `target_price_{h}d`. Never returns.
    42	3. **Target Zones = horizontal lines** at price levels. NEVER say: cones, bands, funnels, confidence intervals.
    43	4. **Probability language:** "ZL has an X% chance of hitting XX.XX by [date]" — derived from Monte Carlo (10k runs) + pinball loss + MAE/accuracy %.
    44	5. **No Inngest. No Vercel Cron.** All scheduling via Supabase pg_cron + `http` extension. Vercel is frontend hosting ONLY.
    45	6. **9 schemas:** mkt, econ, alt, supply, training, forecasts, analytics, ops, vegas. No others.
    46	7. **ProFarmer is mandatory** ($500/month). Rebuilt as Python Playwright scraper, not Node.js Puppeteer.
    47	8. **Training gate:** NEVER start model training without explicit user approval.
    48	9. **Chart is sacred.** REWRITE from scratch using legacy baseline as visual reference — zero modifications to behavior. NEVER copy legacy baseline code.
    49	10. **Landing page is sacred.** REWRITE from scratch using legacy baseline as visual reference — preserve the design identity. NEVER copy legacy baseline code.
    50	11. **ZERO mock data.** No placeholders, no temps, no demo/synthetic/random data anywhere, ever. Empty state until real data flows. This is the HARDEST rule.
    51	12. **ZERO code copying.** Every line of V16 is written fresh. legacy baseline is a visual reference only. Clone-and-clean failed catastrophically — never again.
    52	13. **Cloud Supabase only.** Cloud Supabase is canonical. Supabase CLI is for cloud migrations (`db push`) only. No `supabase status`. No `supabase start`. Local PostgreSQL is permitted only as the AG training-source staging database, never as canonical warehouse or local Supabase replacement.
    53	14. **No hardcoded port 3000.** Dev server port must be checked for availability first.
    54	15. **Design holdoff exception (locked 2026-05-07).** For page parity work, do not redesign. Reproduce locked source visuals exactly.
    55	16. **Locked page authority map (2026-05-07):** Strategy=V16, Vegas Intel=V16, Dashboard=V15, Legislation=V15, Sentiment=V15.
    56	17. **Global layout lock:** Full-width geometry is mandatory on ALL V16 pages. Do not keep narrow/containerized widths.
    57	18. **Pixel parity lock:** Typography, spacing, shadows, border/radius, gradients, hex colors, and all interaction states must match locked source exactly.
    58	19. **Cadence pivot lock:** Most non-price data moves to weekly batch ingest and weekly retraining; hourly chart freshness remains.
    59	20. **GPT content lock:** Sentiment and Legislation pipelines are GPT-driven from approved source feeds with traceable provenance.
    60	21. **AI-first cards lock (2026-05-08):** Dashboard/strategy/sentiment/legislation/vegas cards are primarily AI snapshot driven; avoid request-time external data pulls in API handlers.
    61	22. **Weekly pull lock (2026-05-08):** Non-price card refresh jobs run weekly cadence by default; AG training runs by manual batch trigger only unless explicitly changed.
    62	23. **Source reduction lock (2026-05-08):** Keep only strict high-impact feeds (FOMC/FED, ProFarmer, FRED aggregate series, constrained weather stations, core energy/FX/volatility signals).
    63	24. **Symbol budget lock (2026-05-08):** Maintain a compact universe (target ~20-30 symbols); do not reopen 50+ symbol spread without explicit approval.
    64	
    65	### Process Rules
    66	
    67	1. **Read the migration plan before touching code.** It defines every table, route, job, and phase.
    68	2. **Follow the phase order.** Phase 0 before Phase 1. Phase 1 before Phase 2. No skipping.
    69	3. **Run evaluation gates.** Each phase has specific checks. Don't declare done until they pass.
    70	4. **One task at a time.** Finish what was asked before touching anything else.
    71	5. **No "while I'm here" refactors.** Stay scoped.
    72	6. **Memory first.** Search memory at session start, store decisions immediately.
    73	7. **Brainstorm before building.** Use the superpowers brainstorming skill for any non-trivial feature.
    74	8. **Verify before claiming done.** Use the superpowers verification skill.
    75	9. **Checkpoint before implementation.** For non-trivial planning work, audit repo reality first, structure the plan as numbered decision checkpoints, run one Ralph Loop per checkpoint, and update docs after each locked decision.
    76	
    77	### Security Rules
    78	
    79	1. No `service_role` key exposed to browser — ever.
    80	2. `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` vars contain only anon key and URL.
    81	3. No manual `.env` copying. Use `vercel env pull` exclusively.
    82	4. ProFarmer credentials stay local — never deployed to Vercel.
    83	5. RLS enabled on every table from day one.
    84	6. API keys for data ingestion stored in Supabase Vault — not env vars, not hardcoded.
    85	
    86	---
    87	
    88	## Banned Words
    89	
    90	Never use these in code, comments, UI, or conversation:
    91	
    92	- "cones" / "probability cone"
    93	- "confidence band"
    94	- "funnel"
    95	- "cents/lb" (use "ZL futures contract price" or "price")
    96	- "10 specialists" (there are 11)

   100	## Execution Phases (Summary)
   101	
   102	Full details in the migration plan. Quick reference:
   103	
   104	| Phase   | What                            | Key Deliverable                                                                                     |
   105	| ------- | ------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
   106	| **0**   | Infrastructure foundation       | Supabase cloud + Vercel + shadcn/ui + health route                                                  |
   107	| **1**   | Schema & seed                   | All 9 schemas, RLS, indexes, Gate 2 passes                                                          |
   108	| **1.5** | **All page rewrites**           | All 6 pages rewritten from scratch (legacy baseline visual ref only). Empty state until data wired. |
   109	| **2**   | Read path — chart & live price  | Chart renders with real data from Supabase                                                          |
   110	| **3**   | Landing page completion         | Faithful rewrite of legacy baseline landing design                                                  |
   111	| **4**   | Data ingestion (pg_cron+http)   | ZL daily, intraday, FRED, futures — all via Supabase pg_cron                                        |
   112	| **5**   | Python pipeline rebuild         | Full ML pipeline, local files for intermediates, promote to cloud                                   |
   113	| **6**   | Remaining ingestion + ProFarmer | All data sources feeding via pg_cron+http, ProFarmer Playwright                                     |
   114	| **7**   | Dashboard completion            | Target Zones, drivers, regime, cards — all live                                                     |
   115	| **8**   | Secondary pages wiring          | Sentiment, Legislation, Strategy, Vegas Intel — real data                                           |
   116	| **9**   | Auth & observability            | Supabase Auth, monitoring, Gate 3 passes                                                            |
   117	| **10**  | Parallel validation & cutover   | legacy baseline/V16 parity confirmed, traffic switched                                              |
   118	
   119	---
   120	
   121	## Key Supabase Patterns
   122	
   123	### Connection Strategy
   124	
   125	```
   126	Frontend (reads):      Supabase JS client with anon key + JWT
   127	Data ingestion:        pg_cron + http extension (runs inside Postgres — no external connection)
   128	Python (reads):        psycopg2 pooled connection to cloud (port 6543)
   129	Python (training src): local PostgreSQL staging DB for AG training only
   130	Python (promotes):     psycopg2 direct connection to cloud (port 5432) — validated outputs only
   131	Python (intermediates): local parquet files — never written to any database
   132	```
   133	
   134	### Migration Pattern
   135	
   136	```bash
   137	# Create a new migration
   138	supabase migration new <name>
   139	
   140	# Push directly to cloud (no local Supabase needed)
   141	supabase db push
   142	
   143	# Diff against cloud
   144	supabase db diff --linked
   145	```
   146	
   147	Never do manual DDL on cloud. Migrations are the single source of truth. No `supabase status`, no `supabase start`, and no local Supabase workflow.
   148	
   149	### Data Ingestion Pattern (pg_cron + http extension)
   150	
   151	All data ingestion runs inside Postgres as plpgsql functions:
   152	
   153	1. pg_cron triggers the function on schedule
   154	2. `http_get()` fetches from external API (synchronous, in-transaction)
   155	3. Parse JSON response in plpgsql
   156	4. UPSERT to target table
   157	5. Log to `ops.ingest_run`
   158	6. API keys from Supabase Vault via `current_setting()`
   159	
   160	No Vercel cron routes. No CRON_SECRET. No serverless functions for ingestion.
   161	
   162	### RLS Pattern
   163	
   164	```sql
   165	-- Enable RLS
   166	ALTER TABLE schema.table ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
   167	
   168	-- Authenticated users can read
   169	CREATE POLICY "authenticated_read" ON schema.table
   170	  FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (true);
   171	
   172	-- Only service_role can write
   173	CREATE POLICY "service_role_write" ON schema.table
   174	  FOR ALL TO service_role USING (true);
   175	```
   176	
   177	---
   178	
   179	## Tips From Legacy Baseline Experience
   180	
   181	These are hard-won lessons. Don't repeat them.
   182	
   183	### Data Source Gotchas
   184	
   185	- **FRED daily** only fetches `limit=5` (latest obs). Full history needs `refresh_fred_api.py`.
   186	- **EIA API** has been intermittently down since Mar 2026. Build with graceful fallback.
   187	- **MPOB Palm** needs a valid FAS OpenData API key. The legacy baseline key was wrong (FoodData Central, not FAS).
   188	- **Yahoo Finance v8** downsamples to monthly for large date ranges. Use 1-year windows with `period1/period2`.
   189	- **FAS site** (fas.usda.gov) returns HTTP/2 stream errors. Needs retry logic.
   190	- **UCO/Tallow** prices: no free direct API. Use FRED PPI proxies: `WPU06410132` (Tallow PPI) + `PCU3116133116132` (Rendering PPI).
   191	
   192	### AutoGluon Gotchas
   193	
   194	- `TimeSeriesPredictor.load(path, require_version_match=False)` — version check bug fires even when versions match.
   195	- All covariates are OBSERVED, not KNOWN. This limits Chronos2 effectiveness.
   196	- CPU-only on macOS ARM. Deep learning models (DeepAR, TFT, etc.) are disabled.
   197	- Training the 19-model zoo takes significant time. Always `--dry-run` first.
   198	
   199	### Frontend Gotchas
   200	
   201	- The chart uses `lightweight-charts` (TradingView). Settings are precise — don't modify them.
   202	- `ForecastTargetsPrimitive` is a custom series primitive for drawing horizontal Target Zone lines.
   203	- Pivot labels use format `D(P)`, `D(R1)`, `D(S1)`, `W(P)`, `M(P)`, `Y(P)`.
   204	- The NeuralSphere on the landing page uses Three.js + `head.glb`. Resource-heavy but intentional.
   205	
   206	### Supabase-Specific Tips
   207	
   208	- Use `supabase db diff` to check for schema drift between local and cloud.
   209	- Connection pooler (port 6543) has a statement timeout. Use direct (5432) for long writes.
   210	- `pg_cron` jobs run as the `postgres` role. Make sure your functions/procedures are owned by `postgres`.
   211	- Supabase Auth JWTs expire after 1 hour by default. Configure refresh behavior in the client.
   212	
   213	---
   214	
   215	## Vegas Intel — What Makes It Special
   216	
   217	This page is Kevin's primary sales tool. Key features that must survive:
   218	
   219	1. **Events calendar** — CES, SEMA, March Madness, conventions. Links events to oil demand spikes.
   220	2. **Intel buttons** — AI-powered recommendations for each restaurant account.
   221	3. **AI sales strategy** — Generates personalized pitches using real customer data + real oil consumption volumes.
   222	4. **Customer matching** — API pulls real customer records, matches with event impact predictions.
   223	5. **Fryer tracking** — Equipment lifecycle drives service scheduling recommendations.
   224	
   225	This is not a generic dashboard page. It's a sales intelligence tool that directly drives revenue.
   226

The only thing the two modes differ on is WHO drives — you (Mode A) or the orchestrator subprocess-spawning a CLI agent (Mode B).
.github/skills/quality-playbook/SKILL.md:92:The operator runs `python3 -m bin.run_playbook` themselves (typically because they want batching, headless CI, or to route per-phase work to a different model). The orchestrator at `bin/run_playbook.py` spawns a CLI agent per phase, feeds it the externalized phase prompt, and aggregates the result.
.github/skills/quality-playbook/SKILL.md:102:**Never invoke it script-style** (`python bin/run_playbook.py ...`). The runtime guard exits with `EX_USAGE=64` because relative imports require packaged execution.
.github/skills/quality-playbook/SKILL.md:159:2. **Don't patch QPB source mid-run.** If you encounter a bug in `bin/`, `.github/skills/`, `agents/`, `references/`, `SKILL.md`, `schemas.md`, or `AGENTS.md` during the run, **STOP and report**: name the file:line, describe the failure, propose a fix shape — but do NOT apply the fix. The orchestrator captures a git-SHA baseline at run start and verifies the source tree unchanged at every phase boundary; an autonomous patch fails the gate with a diagnostic naming the modified files. Patches go through Council review, not mid-run improvisation.

.github/skills/quality-playbook/SKILL.md:850:2. **Phase B — Requirement derivation.** Read CONTRACTS.md and documentation. Group related contracts, enrich with user intent, write formal requirements. Write REQ records to `quality/requirements_manifest.json` (source of truth) and render to `quality/REQUIREMENTS.md`. For each requirement, record the `tier` (1–5 per schemas.md §3.1) and — when `tier ∈ {1, 2}` — the `citation` block produced by `bin/reference_docs_ingest` invoking `bin/citation_verifier` per schemas.md §5.4 / §5.5. The LLM does not shell out to `citation_verifier` directly; the excerpt is a product of the ingest pipeline and is re-verified by `quality_gate.py` at gate time. For Tier 3 REQs (code-is-the-spec), cite the source `file:line` in the `description`; citations are for FORMAL_DOC references only and must not appear on Tier 3/4/5 REQs. The tier + citation pair creates the forward link in the traceability chain: reference_docs/cite → requirements → bugs → tests. See the tier/citation framing block later in this step for the full field list and the Tier-1-wins-over-Tier-2 rule.
.github/skills/quality-playbook/SKILL.md:888:   #!/bin/bash
.github/skills/quality-playbook/SKILL.md:961:- **Tier 4** — informal documentation loaded by `bin/reference_docs_ingest.load_tier4_context` from top-level `reference_docs/` (AI chats, design notes, retrospectives).
.github/skills/quality-playbook/SKILL.md:964:For `tier ∈ {1, 2}`, the REQ also carries a `citation` block per `schemas.md` §5 with `document`, `document_sha256`, at least one of `section`/`line`, and a mechanically-extracted `citation_excerpt`. Do NOT write the excerpt by hand. The excerpt is produced at ingest time by `bin/reference_docs_ingest` invoking `bin/citation_verifier` per the deterministic algorithm in `schemas.md` §5.4 (with section resolution per §5.5) — the LLM consumes the excerpt from `formal_docs_manifest.json`; it never shells out to the verifier directly. Ingest-time extraction is how Layer 1 of the hallucination gate works. If you cannot cite a document in `quality/formal_docs_manifest.json` (with hash and locator), the REQ is at most Tier 3. `page`-only locators are diagnostic-only and are never sufficient.
.github/skills/quality-playbook/SKILL.md:968:**Spec-Gap degradation (valid output state).** If `formal_docs_manifest.json` contains zero `FORMAL_DOC` records covering the project's own behavior, every REQ ends up at Tier 3/4/5 and the run degrades gracefully into a Spec Gap Analyzer. Report the meta-finding "0 Tier 1/2 requirements" in the completeness report as a metric, not a failure. Do NOT fabricate citations to make the tier distribution look richer — `quality_gate.py` re-invokes `bin/citation_verifier` (via `extract_excerpt`) per §5.4 at verification time and rejects any Tier 1/2 REQ whose `citation_excerpt` does not byte-equal the fresh extraction (schemas.md §10 invariant #11).
.github/skills/quality-playbook/SKILL.md:980:- **Citation** (required when `tier ∈ {1, 2}`): produced by `bin/reference_docs_ingest` invoking `bin/citation_verifier`; never hand-authored and never invoked directly by the LLM. Shape per schemas.md §5.1.
.github/skills/quality-playbook/SKILL.md:1244:**v1.5.6 instrumentation:** Append `phase_start phase=2` to `quality/run_state.jsonl` now. At phase end, cross-validate by calling `bin/run_state_lib.validate_phase_artifacts(quality_dir, 2)` — it checks the full Generate contract (REQUIREMENTS.md, QUALITY.md, CONTRACTS.md, COVERAGE_MATRIX.md, COMPLETENESS_REPORT.md, RUN_CODE_REVIEW.md, RUN_INTEGRATION_TESTS.md, RUN_SPEC_AUDIT.md, RUN_TDD_TESTS.md, plus one non-empty `quality/test_functional.<ext>`). If validation passes, append `phase_end phase=2`. If it fails, append an `error` event with `recoverable: true` and re-run the missing artifact generation. (BUG-014 fix: pre-v1.5.6 this note referenced the v1.5.5-design triage model that never shipped.)
.github/skills/quality-playbook/SKILL.md:1255:**Phase 2 source-modification guardrail (mandatory — HARD STOP).** Phase 2 writes ONLY into `quality/`. Do NOT create, modify, or delete any file outside the target repo's `quality/` directory — this includes (but is not limited to) `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `README.md`, `bin/**`, `.github/**`, `.claude/**`, `references/**`, `agents/**`, `SKILL.md`, `schemas.md`, source code, or test files. The target repo's `AGENTS.md` is generated by the orchestrator AFTER Phase 6 completes; if you write `AGENTS.md` in Phase 2 you will trip the orchestrator's source-unchanged invariant and abort the run. The 2026-04-30 codex bootstrap test failed exactly this way: a Phase 2 LLM updated the existing `AGENTS.md` (because earlier SKILL.md prose told it to), the source-unchanged gate detected the modification, and the run aborted before Phase 3. If a generated artifact would naturally live outside `quality/`, write it under `quality/` instead and let the orchestrator move or copy it during finalization. The single permitted exception is `quality/PROGRESS.md`, which is itself inside `quality/`.
.github/skills/quality-playbook/SKILL.md:1296:- For Python files with an encoding pragma (`# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-`) or a shebang (`#!/usr/bin/env python`), place the stamp comment *after* the pragma/shebang, not before — pushing it past line 2 causes `SyntaxWarning`.
.github/skills/quality-playbook/SKILL.md:1316:- `quality/formal_docs_manifest.json` — written by `bin/reference_docs_ingest.py` in Phase 1. Do not rewrite.
.github/skills/quality-playbook/SKILL.md:1740:**v1.5.4 contract: `AGENTS.md` is generated by `bin/run_playbook.py` after Phase 6 succeeds, NOT by you during Phase 2.** The orchestrator's `_safe_write_agents_md` helper writes the file to the **target's repo root** (`<target>/AGENTS.md`, not `quality/AGENTS.md`) using the `quality/` artifacts you produced in Phase 2 + the Phase 6 gate verdict as input. The generator carries a `<!-- generated by QPB v… -->` sentinel on the first non-empty line so subsequent runs detect QPB-managed copies.
.github/skills/quality-playbook/SKILL.md:1973:After the main spec audit triage, each Council member runs a per-REQ verdict against every Tier 1/2 REQ's `citation_excerpt`. This is **Layer 2** of the hallucination gate: Layer 1 is the mechanical byte-equality check — `bin/citation_verifier` is invoked by `bin/reference_docs_ingest` at ingest time and re-invoked by `quality_gate.py` at gate time; the LLM never shells out to it directly. Layer 2 is semantic — the reviewer decides whether the excerpt actually supports the requirement as stated, or whether the requirement overreaches what the excerpt says.
.github/skills/quality-playbook/SKILL.md:2112:- `quality/formal_docs_manifest.json` exists (v1.5.3 — written by `bin/reference_docs_ingest.py` in Phase 1; empty `records[]` is valid when no formal docs present)
.github/skills/quality-playbook/SKILL.md:2133:- **#11 — citation excerpt byte-equality.** The gate re-runs `bin/citation_verifier.extract_excerpt` per schemas.md §5.4 on every Tier 1/2 citation and rejects any stored `citation_excerpt` that does not byte-equal the freshly-extracted one. This is the Layer-1 anti-hallucination mechanism — it catches fabricated or paraphrased excerpts even when the locator is real.
.github/skills/quality-playbook/SKILL.md:2219:**v1.5.3 Layer-1 invariants also run here.** `quality_gate.py` additionally enforces schemas.md §10 invariants #1–#18 (summarized in Phase 5 above). In particular, the script re-runs `bin/citation_verifier.extract_excerpt` per schemas.md §5.4 on every Tier 1/2 citation and rejects any stored `citation_excerpt` that does not byte-equal the freshly-extracted output — this is the post-ingest tampering catch. If any Layer-1 invariant fails here, fix the underlying manifest record (not the gate, not the excerpt) and re-run.
.github/skills/quality-playbook/SKILL.md:2342:2. Archive the current `quality/` directory via `bin/run_playbook.archive_previous_run(repo_dir, timestamp)` (or `bin.archive_lib.archive_run()` at Phase 6 success). These snapshot `quality/` into `quality/previous_runs/<timestamp>/quality/` and write the per-run `INDEX.md` plus a `RUN_INDEX.md` row.

--- docs/contracts/engineering-principles.md
# V16 Engineering Principles (Executable)

## Fail-Closed Core
- If safety or data integrity is unclear, stop and mark the item blocked.
- Never assume schema compatibility: verify with migration SQL and table introspection.
- Never claim behavior without command output or file evidence.

## Data Contract-First
- API contracts are derived from schema contracts.
- Every table must have a writer and a reader.
- Every external feed write must include `ingested_at` and source traceability.

## Quant Safety
- No future leakage into features.
- Target labels remain explicit and versioned (`target_price_{h}d`).
- Forecast writes must include model version and feature snapshot metadata.

## Security Baselines
- `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` is server-only.
- Every `/api/cron/*` route must enforce `CRON_SECRET`.
--- docs/contracts/fail-closed-checklist.md
# Fail-Closed Checklist

- [ ] Requirements mapped to authority docs (`AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, migration plan, mistakes doc).
- [ ] Write-set declared before changes.
- [ ] `git status --short` reviewed before and after changes.
- [ ] Typecheck passed: `npx tsc --noEmit`.
- [ ] Build passed: `npm run build`.
- [ ] Targeted route checks executed for touched APIs.
- [ ] If schema touched: migration SQL reviewed and DB validation run.
- [ ] Unknowns explicitly marked and either resolved or blocked.
- [ ] Rollback path documented (git revert + compensating migration).
--- docs/contracts/page-surface.md
# V16 Page Surface Contract

## Required Pages
- `/` Landing page.
- `/dashboard` Chart, live status, target zones, key dashboard cards.
- `/strategy` Posture and contract-impact view.
- `/legislation` Policy and regulation feed.
- `/sentiment` Sentiment and positioning intelligence.
- `/vegas-intel` Vegas operations intelligence.

## Page Authority Lock (2026-05-07)

| Page | Visual Source of Truth |
|---|---|
| `/dashboard` | V15 |
| `/strategy` | V16 |
| `/legislation` | V15 |
| `/sentiment` | V15 |
| `/vegas-intel` | V16 |

--- docs/contracts/security-model.md
# V16 Security Model (Scaffold)

## Access Tiers
- Browser users: authenticated session/JWT with RLS-constrained reads.
- System jobs: service role for controlled writes.

## Route Boundaries
- `/api/cron/*` uses `CRON_SECRET` verification.
- `/api/auth/check` validates active claims.
- Page middleware excludes `/api/*` and applies navigation auth controls.

## Secret Handling
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_*`: URL + publishable key only.
- `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`: server-only usage (`lib/server/supabase-admin.ts`).
- ProFarmer credentials stay off Vercel.

## Required Controls
- RLS enabled for all created tables.
- Ingestion runs logged to `ops.ingest_run`.
- Build/typecheck gates required before merge.

--- scripts/verify/gate1.sh
     1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2	set -euo pipefail
     3	
     4	echo "[Gate1] Supabase foundation verification"
     5	
     6	for cmd in node npm supabase psql python3; do
     7	  command -v "${cmd}" >/dev/null
     8	done
     9	echo "[Gate1] Tooling check passed (node, npm, supabase, psql, python3)"
    10	
    11	if [ -f ".env.local" ]; then
    12	  set -a
    13	  # shellcheck disable=SC1091
    14	  source .env.local
    15	  set +a
    16	  echo "[Gate1] Loaded .env.local"
    17	fi
    18	
    19	DB_URL="${DATABASE_URL:-${SUPABASE_DB_URL:-}}"
    20	if [ -z "${DB_URL}" ]; then
    21	  echo "[Gate1] FAIL: DATABASE_URL (or SUPABASE_DB_URL fallback) must be set" >&2
    22	  exit 1
    23	fi
    24	
    25	# Keep legacy alias for existing Python scripts while standardizing on DATABASE_URL.
    26	if [ -z "${SUPABASE_DB_URL:-}" ]; then
    27	  export SUPABASE_DB_URL="${DB_URL}"
    28	fi
    29	
    30	if [ ! -f ".vercel/project.json" ]; then
    31	  echo "[Gate1] FAIL: .vercel/project.json is missing; project linkage cannot be verified" >&2
    32	  exit 1
    33	fi
    34	
    35	linked_project="$(
    36	  node -e '
    37	    const fs = require("fs");
    38	    const data = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(".vercel/project.json", "utf8"));
    39	    process.stdout.write(data.projectName ?? "");
    40	  '
    41	)"
    42	if [ "${linked_project}" != "zinc-fusion-v16" ]; then
    43	  echo "[Gate1] FAIL: linked Vercel project is '${linked_project}', expected 'zinc-fusion-v16'" >&2
    44	  exit 1
    45	fi
    46	echo "[Gate1] Vercel linkage check passed (${linked_project})"
    47	
    48	schema_count="$(psql "${DB_URL}" -X -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1 -Atqc "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_namespace WHERE nspname IN ('mkt','econ','alt','supply','training','forecasts','analytics','ops','vegas');")"
    49	if [ "${schema_count}" -ne 9 ]; then
    50	  echo "[Gate1] FAIL: expected 9 V16 schemas, found ${schema_count}" >&2
    51	  exit 1
    52	fi
    53	echo "[Gate1] Cloud schema reachability passed (9 schemas visible)"
    54	
    55	source_count="$(psql "${DB_URL}" -X -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1 -Atqc "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ops.source_registry;")"
    56	if [ "${source_count}" -lt 6 ]; then
    57	  echo "[Gate1] FAIL: ops.source_registry has ${source_count} rows (expected at least 6 seeded sources)" >&2
    58	  exit 1
    59	fi
    60	echo "[Gate1] Source registry check passed (${source_count} rows)"
    61	
    62	BASE_URL="${BASE_URL:-}"
    63	if [ -n "${BASE_URL}" ]; then
    64	  echo "[Gate1] Runtime health check against ${BASE_URL}/api/health"
    65	  body_file="$(mktemp)"
    66	  status="$(curl -sS -o "${body_file}" -w "%{http_code}" "${BASE_URL}/api/health")"
    67	  if [ "${status}" -ne 200 ]; then
    68	    echo "[Gate1] FAIL: /api/health returned status ${status}" >&2
    69	    rm -f "${body_file}"
    70	    exit 1
    71	  fi
    72	  node -e '
    73	    const fs = require("fs");
    74	    const payload = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(process.argv[1], "utf8"));
    75	    if (!(payload && payload.ok === true && payload.dbReachable === true)) {
    76	      process.exit(1);
    77	    }
    78	  ' "${body_file}" || {
    79	    echo "[Gate1] FAIL: /api/health payload missing ok=true and dbReachable=true" >&2
    80	    rm -f "${body_file}"
    81	    exit 1
    82	  }
    83	  rm -f "${body_file}"
    84	  echo "[Gate1] Runtime health check passed"
    85	else
    86	  echo "[Gate1] BASE_URL not set; runtime /api/health check skipped"
    87	fi
    88	
    89	echo "[Gate1] Running Python connectivity validation"
    90	PYTHONPATH=python python3 python/test_connection.py
    91	
    92	echo "Gate1 verification complete"
--- scripts/verify/gate2.sh
     1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2	set -euo pipefail
     3	
     4	echo "[Gate2] Schema integrity verification"
     5	
     6	if [ -f ".env.local" ]; then
     7	  set -a
     8	  # shellcheck disable=SC1091
     9	  source .env.local
    10	  set +a
    11	  echo "[Gate2] Loaded .env.local"
    12	fi
    13	
    14	DB_URL="${DATABASE_URL:-${SUPABASE_DB_URL:-}}"
    15	if [ -z "${DB_URL}" ]; then
    16	  echo "[Gate2] FAIL: DATABASE_URL (or SUPABASE_DB_URL fallback) must be set" >&2
    17	  exit 1
    18	fi
    19	
    20	SCHEMAS="'mkt','econ','alt','supply','training','forecasts','analytics','ops','vegas'"
    21	
    22	run_sql_scalar() {
    23	  local sql="$1"
    24	  psql "${DB_URL}" -X -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1 -Atqc "${sql}"
    25	}
    26	
    27	assert_eq() {
    28	  local label="$1"
    29	  local actual="$2"
    30	  local expected="$3"
    31	  if [ "${actual}" -ne "${expected}" ]; then
    32	    echo "[Gate2] FAIL: ${label} expected ${expected}, got ${actual}" >&2
    33	    exit 1
    34	  fi
    35	  echo "[Gate2] PASS: ${label} = ${actual}"
    36	}
    37	
    38	assert_zero() {
    39	  local label="$1"
    40	  local actual="$2"
    41	  assert_eq "${label}" "${actual}" 0
    42	}
    43	
    44	assert_ge() {
    45	  local label="$1"
    46	  local actual="$2"
    47	  local min="$3"
    48	  if [ "${actual}" -lt "${min}" ]; then
    49	    echo "[Gate2] FAIL: ${label} expected >= ${min}, got ${actual}" >&2
    50	    exit 1
    51	  fi
    52	  echo "[Gate2] PASS: ${label} = ${actual} (>= ${min})"
    53	}
    54	
    55	schema_count="$(run_sql_scalar "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_namespace WHERE nspname IN (${SCHEMAS});")"
    56	assert_eq "schema count" "${schema_count}" 9
    57	
    58	table_count="$(run_sql_scalar "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname IN (${SCHEMAS});")"
    59	assert_ge "table count across 9 schemas" "${table_count}" 80
    60	
    61	missing_pk_count="$(run_sql_scalar "
    62	SELECT COUNT(*)
    63	FROM pg_class c
    64	JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace
    65	WHERE c.relkind = 'r'
    66	  AND n.nspname IN (${SCHEMAS})
    67	  AND NOT EXISTS (
    68	    SELECT 1
    69	    FROM pg_constraint con
    70	    WHERE con.conrelid = c.oid
    71	      AND con.contype = 'p'
    72	  );
    73	")"
    74	assert_zero "tables missing primary key" "${missing_pk_count}"
    75	
    76	missing_created_at_count="$(run_sql_scalar "
    77	SELECT COUNT(*)
    78	FROM pg_tables t
    79	WHERE t.schemaname IN (${SCHEMAS})
    80	  AND NOT EXISTS (
    81	    SELECT 1
    82	    FROM information_schema.columns c
    83	    WHERE c.table_schema = t.schemaname
    84	      AND c.table_name = t.tablename
    85	      AND c.column_name = 'created_at'
    86	  );
    87	")"
    88	assert_zero "tables missing created_at" "${missing_created_at_count}"
    89	
    90	missing_ingested_at_count="$(run_sql_scalar "
    91	SELECT COUNT(*)
    92	FROM pg_tables t
    93	WHERE t.schemaname IN (${SCHEMAS})
    94	  AND NOT EXISTS (
    95	    SELECT 1
    96	    FROM information_schema.columns c
    97	    WHERE c.table_schema = t.schemaname
    98	      AND c.table_name = t.tablename
    99	      AND c.column_name = 'ingested_at'
   100	  );
   101	")"
   102	assert_zero "tables missing ingested_at" "${missing_ingested_at_count}"
   103	
   104	rls_disabled_count="$(run_sql_scalar "
   105	SELECT COUNT(*)
   106	FROM pg_class c
   107	JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace
   108	WHERE c.relkind = 'r'
   109	  AND n.nspname IN (${SCHEMAS})
   110	  AND c.relrowsecurity = false;
   111	")"
   112	assert_zero "tables with RLS disabled" "${rls_disabled_count}"
   113	
   114	tables_without_policy_count="$(run_sql_scalar "
   115	SELECT COUNT(*)
   116	FROM (
   117	  SELECT c.oid
   118	  FROM pg_class c
   119	  JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace
   120	  LEFT JOIN pg_policy p ON p.polrelid = c.oid
   121	  WHERE c.relkind = 'r'
   122	    AND n.nspname IN (${SCHEMAS})
   123	  GROUP BY c.oid
   124	  HAVING COUNT(p.oid) = 0
   125	) policy_gap;
   126	")"
   127	assert_zero "tables without any RLS policy" "${tables_without_policy_count}"
   128	
   129	missing_required_index_count="$(run_sql_scalar "
   130	WITH target_columns AS (
   131	  SELECT
   132	    n.nspname AS schema_name,
   133	    c.relname AS table_name,
   134	    a.attname AS column_name
   135	  FROM pg_class c
   136	  JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace
   137	  JOIN pg_attribute a ON a.attrelid = c.oid
   138	  WHERE c.relkind = 'r'
   139	    AND n.nspname IN (${SCHEMAS})
   140	    AND a.attnum > 0
   141	    AND NOT a.attisdropped
   142	    AND a.attname IN (
   143	      'bucket_ts',
   144	      'observation_date',
   145	      'trade_date',
   146	      'event_date',
   147	      'forecast_date',
   148	      'ingested_at',
   149	      'created_at'
   150	    )
   151	),
   152	indexed_columns AS (
   153	  SELECT DISTINCT
   154	    n.nspname AS schema_name,
   155	    c.relname AS table_name,
   156	    a.attname AS column_name
   157	  FROM pg_index i
   158	  JOIN pg_class c ON c.oid = i.indrelid
   159	  JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace
   160	  JOIN pg_attribute a ON a.attrelid = c.oid
   161	  WHERE c.relkind = 'r'
   162	    AND n.nspname IN (${SCHEMAS})
   163	    AND i.indisvalid
   164	    AND a.attnum = ANY(i.indkey)
   165	)
   166	SELECT COUNT(*)
   167	FROM target_columns tc
   168	LEFT JOIN indexed_columns ic
   169	  ON ic.schema_name = tc.schema_name
   170	 AND ic.table_name = tc.table_name
   171	 AND ic.column_name = tc.column_name
   172	WHERE ic.schema_name IS NULL;
   173	")"
   174	assert_zero "required timestamp/date columns missing indexes" "${missing_required_index_count}"
   175	
   176	ingest_run_table_count="$(run_sql_scalar "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema = 'ops' AND table_name = 'ingest_run';")"
   177	assert_eq "ops.ingest_run existence count" "${ingest_run_table_count}" 1
   178	
   179	source_registry_seed_count="$(run_sql_scalar "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ops.source_registry;")"
   180	assert_ge "ops.source_registry rows" "${source_registry_seed_count}" 6
   181	
   182	echo "Gate2 verification complete"
--- scripts/verify/gate3.sh
     1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2	set -euo pipefail
     3	
     4	echo "[Gate3] Auth/security scaffold checks"
     5	rg -n "runCronHandler" app/api/cron -g 'route.ts' >/dev/null
     6	rg -n "SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY" lib/server/supabase-admin.ts >/dev/null
     7	rg -n "startsWith\(\"/api/\"\)" lib/supabase/proxy.ts >/dev/null
     8	
     9	BASE_URL="${BASE_URL:-}"
    10	if [ -n "${BASE_URL}" ]; then
    11	  echo "[Gate3] Runtime auth route check against ${BASE_URL}"
    12	  body_file="$(mktemp)"
    13	  status="$(curl -sS -o "${body_file}" -w "%{http_code}" "${BASE_URL}/api/auth/check")"
    14	
    15	  case "${status}" in
    16	    401|503)
    17	      echo "[Gate3] /api/auth/check fail-closed status: ${status}"
    18	      ;;
    19	    200)
    20	      node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const p=process.argv[1]; const j=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(p,'utf8')); if (!(j && j.authenticated === true)) process.exit(1);" "${body_file}" || {
    21	        echo "[Gate3] 200 response without authenticated=true is not fail-closed" >&2
    22	        rm -f "${body_file}"
    23	        exit 1
    24	      }
    25	      echo "[Gate3] /api/auth/check authenticated runtime path verified"
    26	      ;;
    27	    *)
    28	      echo "[Gate3] unexpected /api/auth/check status: ${status}" >&2
    29	      rm -f "${body_file}"
    30	      exit 1
    31	      ;;
    32	  esac
    33	  rm -f "${body_file}"
    34	else
    35	  echo "[Gate3] BASE_URL not set; runtime auth route check skipped"
    36	fi
    37	
    38	echo "Gate3 scaffold checks complete"

--- scripts/verify/gate6.sh
     1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2	set -euo pipefail
     3	
     4	BASELINE_URL="${BASELINE_URL:-}"
     5	TARGET_URL="${TARGET_URL:-http://localhost:4010}"
     6	
     7	if [ -z "${BASELINE_URL}" ]; then
     8	  echo "[Gate6] BASELINE_URL not set; SKIP parity checks (scaffold mode)"
     9	  exit 0
    10	fi
    11	
    12	if ! command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    13	  echo "[Gate6] jq is required for parity verification" >&2
    14	  exit 1
    15	fi
    16	
    17	artifact_dir="$(mktemp -d)"
    18	trap 'rm -rf "${artifact_dir}"' EXIT
    19	
    20	export BASELINE_URL
    21	export TARGET_URL
    22	export PARITY_ARTIFACT_DIR="${artifact_dir}"
    23	
    24	echo "[Gate6] Fetching parity payloads"
    25	./scripts/validate/parity-zl.sh
    26	./scripts/validate/parity-dashboard.sh
    27	
    28	shape_signature='
    29	  def sig:
    30	    if type == "object" then
    31	      {
    32	        type: "object",
    33	        keys: (keys_unsorted | sort),
    34	        fields: (to_entries | map({key, value: (.value | sig)}) | sort_by(.key))
    35	      }
    36	    elif type == "array" then
    37	      {
    38	        type: "array",
    39	        items: (map(sig) | unique)
    40	      }
    41	    else
    42	      {
    43	        type: type
    44	      }
    45	    end;
    46	  sig
    47	'
    48	
    49	compare_payload() {
    50	  label="$1"
    51	  baseline_file="$2"
    52	  target_file="$3"
    53	  required_filter="$4"
    54	
    55	  jq -e "${required_filter}" "${baseline_file}" >/dev/null
    56	  jq -e "${required_filter}" "${target_file}" >/dev/null
    57	
    58	  baseline_signature="$(jq -c "${shape_signature}" "${baseline_file}")"
    59	  target_signature="$(jq -c "${shape_signature}" "${target_file}")"
    60	
    61	  if [ "${baseline_signature}" != "${target_signature}" ]; then
    62	    echo "[Gate6] parity shape mismatch for ${label}" >&2
    63	    echo "[Gate6] baseline: ${baseline_signature}" >&2
    64	    echo "[Gate6] target:   ${target_signature}" >&2
    65	    exit 1
    66	  fi
    67	}
    68	
    69	echo "[Gate6] Verifying /api/zl/price-1d"
    70	compare_payload \
    71	  "/api/zl/price-1d" \
    72	  "${artifact_dir}/baseline-api-zl-price-1d.json" \
    73	  "${artifact_dir}/target-api-zl-price-1d.json" \
    74	  'type == "object"
    75	   and has("ok") and (.ok | type == "boolean")
    76	   and has("data") and (.data | type == "array")
    77	   and has("asOf") and (.asOf | type == "string")
    78	   and has("source") and (.source | type == "string")
    79	   and has("warning") and (.warning | type == "string")
    80	   and all(.data[]?; type == "object"
    81	     and has("symbol") and (.symbol | type == "string")
    82	     and has("tradeDate") and (.tradeDate | type == "string")
    83	     and has("open") and (.open | type == "number")
    84	     and has("high") and (.high | type == "number")
    85	     and has("low") and (.low | type == "number")
    86	     and has("close") and (.close | type == "number")
    87	     and has("volume") and (.volume | type == "number"))'
    88	
    89	echo "[Gate6] Verifying /api/zl/live"
    90	compare_payload \
    91	  "/api/zl/live" \
    92	  "${artifact_dir}/baseline-api-zl-live.json" \
    93	  "${artifact_dir}/target-api-zl-live.json" \
    94	  'type == "object"
    95	   and has("ok") and (.ok | type == "boolean")
    96	   and has("data")
    97	   and has("asOf") and (.asOf | type == "string")
    98	   and has("source") and (.source | type == "string")
    99	   and has("warning") and (.warning | type == "string")
   100	   and (
   101	     .data == null
   102	     or (
   103	       .data | type == "object"
   104	       and has("symbol") and (.symbol | type == "string")
   105	       and has("price") and (.price | type == "number")
   106	       and has("observedAt") and (.observedAt | type == "string")
   107	     )
   108	   )'
   109	
   110	echo "[Gate6] Verifying /api/zl/target-zones"
   111	compare_payload \
   112	  "/api/zl/target-zones" \
   113	  "${artifact_dir}/baseline-api-zl-target-zones.json" \
   114	  "${artifact_dir}/target-api-zl-target-zones.json" \
   115	  'type == "object"
   116	   and has("ok") and (.ok | type == "boolean")
   117	   and has("data") and (.data | type == "array")
   118	   and has("asOf") and (.asOf | type == "string")
   119	   and has("source") and (.source | type == "string")
   120	   and has("warning") and (.warning | type == "string")
   121	   and all(.data[]?; type == "object"
   122	     and has("horizonDays") and (.horizonDays | type == "number")
   123	     and has("p30") and (.p30 | type == "number")
   124	     and has("p50") and (.p50 | type == "number")
   125	     and has("p70") and (.p70 | type == "number")
   126	     and has("generatedAt") and (.generatedAt | type == "string"))'
   127	
   128	echo "[Gate6] Verifying /api/dashboard/metrics"
   129	compare_payload \
   130	  "/api/dashboard/metrics" \
   131	  "${artifact_dir}/baseline-api-dashboard-metrics.json" \
   132	  "${artifact_dir}/target-api-dashboard-metrics.json" \
   133	  'type == "object"
   134	   and has("ok") and (.ok | type == "boolean")
   135	   and has("data") and (.data | type == "array")
   136	   and has("asOf") and (.asOf | type == "string")
   137	   and has("source") and (.source | type == "string")
   138	   and has("warning") and (.warning | type == "string")
   139	   and all(.data[]?; type == "object"
   140	     and has("key") and (.key | type == "string")
   141	     and has("label") and (.label | type == "string")
   142	     and has("value") and (.value | type == "number"))'
   143	
   144	echo "[Gate6] Verifying /api/dashboard/drivers"
   145	compare_payload \
   146	  "/api/dashboard/drivers" \
   147	  "${artifact_dir}/baseline-api-dashboard-drivers.json" \
   148	  "${artifact_dir}/target-api-dashboard-drivers.json" \
   149	  'type == "object"
   150	   and has("ok") and (.ok | type == "boolean")
   151	   and has("data") and (.data | type == "array")
   152	   and has("asOf") and (.asOf | type == "string")
   153	   and has("source") and (.source | type == "string")
   154	   and has("warning") and (.warning | type == "string")
   155	   and all(.data[]?; type == "object"
   156	     and has("factor") and (.factor | type == "string")
   157	     and has("contribution") and (.contribution | type == "number")
   158	     and has("confidence") and (.confidence | type == "number"))'
   159	
   160	echo "[Gate6] Verifying /api/dashboard/regime"
   161	compare_payload \
   162	  "/api/dashboard/regime" \
   163	  "${artifact_dir}/baseline-api-dashboard-regime.json" \
   164	  "${artifact_dir}/target-api-dashboard-regime.json" \
   165	  'type == "object"
   166	   and has("ok") and (.ok | type == "boolean")
   167	   and has("data")
   168	   and has("asOf") and (.asOf | type == "string")
   169	   and has("source") and (.source | type == "string")
   170	   and has("warning") and (.warning | type == "string")
   171	   and (
   172	     .data == null
   173	     or (
   174	       .data | type == "object"
   175	       and has("regime") and (.regime | type == "string")
   176	       and has("confidence") and (.confidence | type == "number")
   177	       and has("updatedAt") and (.updatedAt | type == "string")
   178	     )
   179	   )'
   180	
   181	echo "Gate6 parity checks complete"

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba lib/supabase/proxy.ts | sed -n '1,300p'; nl -ba proxy.ts | sed -n '1,220p'" in /Users/zincdigital/mnt/SatechiHub/ZINC-FUSION-V16

     1	import { createServerClient } from "@supabase/ssr";
     2	import { NextResponse, type NextRequest } from "next/server";
     3	import { hasEnvVars } from "../utils";
     4	
     5	function isPublicPath(pathname: string): boolean {
     6	  // Landing page
     7	  if (pathname === "/") return true;
     8	  // All auth flows (login, sign-up, callback, confirm, error, etc.)
     9	  if (pathname.startsWith("/auth/")) return true;
    10	  // Public health check endpoint for uptime monitoring
    11	  if (pathname === "/api/health") return true;
    12	  return false;
    13	}
    14	
    15	function isCronPath(pathname: string): boolean {
    16	  return pathname.startsWith("/api/cron/");
    17	}
    18	
    19	function buildLoginRedirect(request: NextRequest): NextResponse {
    20	  const url = request.nextUrl.clone();
    21	  const next = `${request.nextUrl.pathname}${request.nextUrl.search}`;
    22	  url.pathname = "/auth/login";
    23	  url.searchParams.set("next", next);
    24	  return NextResponse.redirect(url);
    25	}
    26	
    27	export async function updateSession(request: NextRequest) {
    28	  const { pathname } = request.nextUrl;
    29	
    30	  let supabaseResponse = NextResponse.next({
    31	    request,
    32	  });
    33	
    34	  // Public splash and auth flows remain reachable without a session.
    35	  if (isPublicPath(pathname)) {
    36	    return supabaseResponse;
    37	  }
    38	
    39	  // Cron routes are machine-to-machine; they enforce CRON_SECRET per route.
    40	  if (isCronPath(pathname)) {
    41	    return supabaseResponse;
    42	  }
    43	
    44	  // Fail closed if auth env is missing for protected surfaces.
    45	  if (!hasEnvVars) {
    46	    if (pathname.startsWith("/api/")) {
    47	      return NextResponse.json(
    48	        { ok: false, error: "Supabase auth env vars are not configured" },
    49	        { status: 503 },
    50	      );
    51	    }
    52	
    53	    return buildLoginRedirect(request);
    54	  }
    55	
    56	  // With Fluid compute, don't put this client in a global environment
    57	  // variable. Always create a new one on each request.
    58	  const supabase = createServerClient(
    59	    process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,
    60	    process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY!,
    61	    {
    62	      cookies: {
    63	        getAll() {
    64	          return request.cookies.getAll();
    65	        },
    66	        setAll(cookiesToSet) {
    67	          cookiesToSet.forEach(({ name, value }) =>
    68	            request.cookies.set(name, value),
    69	          );
    70	          supabaseResponse = NextResponse.next({
    71	            request,
    72	          });
    73	          cookiesToSet.forEach(({ name, value, options }) =>
    74	            supabaseResponse.cookies.set(name, value, options),
    75	          );
    76	        },
    77	      },
    78	    },
    79	  );
    80	
    81	  // Do not run code between createServerClient and
    82	  // supabase.auth.getClaims(). A simple mistake could make it very hard to debug
    83	  // issues with users being randomly logged out.
    84	
    85	  // IMPORTANT: If you remove getClaims() and you use server-side rendering
    86	  // with the Supabase client, your users may be randomly logged out.
    87	  const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.getClaims();
    88	  const user = data?.claims;
    89	
    90	  if (error || !user) {
    91	    if (pathname.startsWith("/api/")) {
    92	      return NextResponse.json(
    93	        { ok: false, error: "Authentication required" },
    94	        { status: 401 },
    95	      );
    96	    }
    97	
    98	    return buildLoginRedirect(request);
    99	  }
   100	
   101	  // IMPORTANT: You *must* return the supabaseResponse object as it is.
   102	  // If you're creating a new response object with NextResponse.next() make sure to:
   103	  // 1. Pass the request in it, like so:
   104	  //    const myNewResponse = NextResponse.next({ request })
   105	  // 2. Copy over the cookies, like so:
   106	  //    myNewResponse.cookies.setAll(supabaseResponse.cookies.getAll())
   107	  // 3. Change the myNewResponse object to fit your needs, but avoid changing
   108	  //    the cookies!
   109	  // 4. Finally:
   110	  //    return myNewResponse
   111	  // If this is not done, you may be causing the browser and server to go out
   112	  // of sync and terminate the user's session prematurely!
   113	
   114	  return supabaseResponse;
   115	}
     1	import { updateSession } from "@/lib/supabase/proxy";
     2	import { type NextRequest } from "next/server";
     3	
     4	export async function proxy(request: NextRequest) {
     5	  return await updateSession(request);
     6	}

    11	     * Match all request paths except:

    14	     * - favicon.ico (favicon file)

    17	     */
    18	    "/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico|.*\\.(?:svg|png|jpg|jpeg|gif|webp)$).*)",
    19	  ],

     9	
    10	type BiasTone = "bullish" | "bearish" | "neutral"
    11	
    12	type SentimentCardContent = Pick<AiCardContent, "title" | "body"> & Partial<AiCardContent>
    13	
    14	type SentimentCards = {
    15	  narratives: SentimentCardContent[]
    16	  positioningFlow: SentimentCardContent

    18	}

    20	function normalizeBias(raw: string): BiasTone {
    21	  const value = raw.trim().toLowerCase()
    22	  if (value.includes("bull")) return "bullish"
    23	  if (value.includes("bear")) return "bearish"
    24	  return "neutral"
    25	}
    26	
    27	function biasStyle(tone: BiasTone): { label: string; chip: string; text: string } {
    28	  if (tone === "bullish") {
    29	    return {
    30	      label: "Bullish",
    31	      chip: "bg-emerald-500/10 border-emerald-500/20",
    32	      text: "text-emerald-400",
    33	    }
    34	  }
    35	  if (tone === "bearish") {
    36	    return {
    37	      label: "Bearish",
    38	      chip: "bg-red-500/10 border-red-500/20",
    39	      text: "text-red-400",
    40	    }
    41	  }
    42	  return {
    43	    label: "Neutral",
    44	    chip: "bg-slate-500/10 border-slate-500/20",
    45	    text: "text-slate-300",
    46	  }
    47	}
    48	
    49	function scoreLabel(score: number): string {
    50	  if (score >= 25) return "Positive Momentum"
    51	  if (score <= -25) return "Defensive Momentum"
    52	  return "Balanced Momentum"
    53	}
    54	
    55	function scoreTextColor(score: number): string {
    56	  if (score >= 25) return "text-emerald-400"
    57	  if (score <= -25) return "text-red-400"
    58	  return "text-amber-400"
    59	}
    60	
    61	function formatTimestamp(value: string): string {
    62	  const parsed = new Date(value)
    63	  if (Number.isNaN(parsed.getTime())) return value
    64	  return parsed.toLocaleString()
    65	}
    66

    68	  title,
    69	  value,

    74	  value: string | number

    85	      <div className="text-xs text-slate-500 leading-relaxed">{subtext}</div>
    86	    </div>
    87	  )
    88	}

    90	export default function SentimentPage() {
    91	  const [overview, setOverview] = useState<SentimentOverview | null>(null)
    92	  const [live, setLive] = useState<ZlLivePrice | null>(null)
    93	  const [cards, setCards] = useState<SentimentCards | null>(null)
    94	  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true)
    95	
    96	  useEffect(() => {
    97	    let active = true
    98	
    99	    async function load() {
   100	      try {
   101	        const [overviewRes, liveRes] = await Promise.all([
   102	          fetch("/api/sentiment/overview", { cache: "no-store" }),
   103	          fetch("/api/zl/live", { cache: "no-store" }),
   104	        ])
   105	
   106	        const overviewBody = (await overviewRes.json()) as ApiEnvelope<SentimentOverview | null> & {
   107	          cards?: SentimentCards
   108	          error?: string
   109	        }
   110	        const liveBody = (await liveRes.json()) as ApiEnvelope<ZlLivePrice | null> & {
   111	          error?: string
   112	        }
   113	
   114	        if (!active) return
   115	
   116	        if (overviewRes.ok && overviewBody.ok && overviewBody.data) {
   117	          setOverview(overviewBody.data)
   118	          setCards(overviewBody.cards ?? null)
   119	        } else {
   120	          setOverview(null)
   121	          setCards(null)
   122	        }
   123	
   124	        if (liveRes.ok && liveBody.ok && liveBody.data) {
   125	          setLive(liveBody.data)
   126	        } else {
   127	          setLive(null)
   128	        }
   129	      } catch {
   130	        if (active) {
   131	          setOverview(null)
   132	          setLive(null)
   133	          setCards(null)
   134	        }
   135	      } finally {
   136	        if (active) setLoading(false)
   137	      }
   138	    }
   139	
   140	    void load()
   141	    return () => {
   142	      active = false
   143	    }
   144	  }, [])
   145	
   146	  const sentimentScore = overview?.sentimentScore ?? 0
   147	  const clampedScore = Math.max(-100, Math.min(100, sentimentScore))
   148	  const meterPercent = ((clampedScore + 100) / 200) * 100
   149	  const cotTone = normalizeBias(overview?.cotBias ?? "neutral")
   150	  const cot = biasStyle(cotTone)
   151	  const scoreLabelText = scoreLabel(clampedScore)
   152	
   153	  const narrativeItems = useMemo(() => {
   154	    if (cards?.narratives?.length) return cards.narratives
   155	    return [
   156	      {
   157	        title: "Macro Narrative",
   158	        body: "Hard stop: macro narrative unavailable because verified sentiment inputs were not returned.",
   159	      },
   160	      {
   161	        title: "Flow Narrative",
   162	        body: "Hard stop: flow narrative unavailable because verified sentiment inputs were not returned.",
   163	      },
   164	      {
   165	        title: "Procurement Narrative",
   166	        body: "Hard stop: procurement narrative unavailable because verified sentiment inputs were not returned.",
   167	      },
   168	    ]
   169	  }, [cards])
   170	
   171	  return (
   172	    <BackendShell>
   173	      <div className="w-full space-y-8">
   174	        <header className="flex flex-col md:flex-row justify-between items-start md:items-center gap-4 pb-4 border-b border-white/5">
   175	          <div>
   176	            <div className="flex items-center gap-3 mb-1">
   177	              <Brain className="w-8 h-8" />
   178	              <h1 className="text-3xl md:text-5xl font-bold tracking-tight text-white">
   179	                Market Psychology
   180	              </h1>
   181	            </div>
   182	            <p className="text-slate-400 text-sm font-mono">
   183	              Quantitative sentiment, narrative clustering, and positioning context
   184	            </p>
   185	          </div>
   186	          <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
   187	            <span className={`px-3 py-1.5 rounded-full text-xs font-bold uppercase tracking-wider border ${cot.chip} ${cot.text}`}>
   188	              CoT Bias {cot.label}
   189	            </span>
   190	            {!loading && overview?.updatedAt && (
   191	              <span className="text-xs text-slate-500 font-mono">
   192	                Updated {formatTimestamp(overview.updatedAt)}
   193	              </span>
   194	            )}
   195	          </div>
   196	        </header>
   197	
   198	        <div className="grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-4">
   199	          <MetricCard
   200	            title="AI Refresh"
   201	            value={overview?.updatedAt ? formatTimestamp(overview.updatedAt) : "—"}
   202	            subtext="Daily 07:00 ET target refresh cadence"
   203	            icon={Clock3}
   204	          />
   205	          <MetricCard
   206	            title="Sentiment Score"
   207	            value={overview ? `${clampedScore > 0 ? "+" : ""}${clampedScore}` : "—"}
   208	            subtext="Pipeline-backed aggregate score"
   209	            icon={TrendingUp}
   210	          />
   211	          <MetricCard
   212	            title="CoT Bias"
   213	            value={overview?.cotBias ?? "—"}
   214	            subtext="Latest managed-money positioning bias"
   215	            icon={Activity}
   216	          />
   217	          <MetricCard
   218	            title="ZL Live"
   219	            value={live?.price != null ? live.price.toFixed(2) : "—"}
   220	            subtext={live?.observedAt ? `Observed ${formatTimestamp(live.observedAt)}` : "Awaiting live price feed"}
   221	            icon={Brain}
   222	          />

     1	"use client"
     2	
     3	import { useEffect, useMemo, useState } from "react"
     4	import { Activity, Building2, Gavel, Newspaper, Radio, Tag } from "lucide-react"
     5

     9	
    10	type LegislationCards = {
    11	  feedSummary: AiCardContent
    12	  sourcePressure: AiCardContent
    13	  tagPressure: AiCardContent
    14	}
    15	
    16	function safeDate(value: string): Date | null {
    17	  const parsed = new Date(value)
    18	  return Number.isNaN(parsed.getTime()) ? null : parsed
    19	}
    20	
    21	function formatDisplayDate(value: string): string {
    22	  const parsed = safeDate(value)
    23	  return parsed ? parsed.toLocaleDateString() : value
    24	}
    25	
    26	function hoursAgo(value: string, nowMs: number | null): string {
    27	  const parsed = safeDate(value)
    28	  if (!parsed || nowMs === null) return "Unknown"
    29	  const deltaMs = nowMs - parsed.getTime()
    30	  if (deltaMs < 0) return "Now"
    31	  if (deltaMs < 3_600_000) return "Now"
    32	  const hours = Math.floor(deltaMs / 3_600_000)
    33	  if (hours < 24) return `${hours}h ago`
    34	  return `${Math.floor(hours / 24)}d ago`
    35	}
    36	
    37	function MetricCard({
    38	  title,
    39	  value,
    40	  subtext,
    41	  icon: Icon,
    42	}: {
    43	  title: string
    44	  value: string | number
    45	  subtext: string
    46	  icon: typeof Activity
    47	}) {

    50	      <div className="flex items-start justify-between gap-2 mb-3">
    51	        <div className="text-xs text-white uppercase tracking-widest font-bold">

    53	        </div>
    54	        <Icon className="w-4 h-4 text-slate-500" />
    55	      </div>
    56	      <div className="text-3xl font-bold font-mono text-white mb-1">{value}</div>
    57	      <div className="text-xs text-slate-500 leading-relaxed">{subtext}</div>
    58	    </div>
    59	  )
    60	}
    61	
    62	export default function LegislationPage() {
    63	  const [items, setItems] = useState<LegislationItem[]>([])
    64	  const [cards, setCards] = useState<LegislationCards | null>(null)
    65	  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true)
    66	  const [clientNow, setClientNow] = useState<number | null>(null)
    67	
    68	  useEffect(() => {
    69	    let active = true
    70	
    71	    async function load() {
    72	      try {
    73	        const res = await fetch("/api/legislation/feed", { cache: "no-store" })
    74	        const body = (await res.json()) as ApiEnvelope<LegislationItem[]> & {
    75	          cards?: LegislationCards
    76	          error?: string
    77	        }
    78	        if (!active) return
    79	        if (res.ok && body.ok && Array.isArray(body.data)) {
    80	          setItems(body.data)
    81	          setCards(body.cards ?? null)
    82	        } else {
    83	          setItems([])
    84	          setCards(null)
    85	        }
    86	      } catch {
    87	        if (active) {
    88	          setItems([])
    89	          setCards(null)
    90	        }
    91	      } finally {
    92	        if (active) setLoading(false)
    93	      }
    94	    }
    95	
    96	    void load()
    97	    return () => {
    98	      active = false
    99	    }
   100	  }, [])
   101	
   102	  useEffect(() => {
   103	    setClientNow(Date.now())
   104	  }, [])
   105	
   106	  const analytics = useMemo(() => {
   107	    const timestamps = items
   108	      .map((item) => safeDate(item.publishedAt)?.getTime() ?? null)
   109	      .filter((ts): ts is number => ts !== null)
   110	
   111	    const last24h =
   112	      clientNow === null ? 0 : timestamps.filter((ts) => clientNow - ts <= 86_400_000).length
   113	    const last7d =
   114	      clientNow === null ? 0 : timestamps.filter((ts) => clientNow - ts <= 604_800_000).length
   115	    const dailyBaseline = last7d > 0 ? last7d / 7 : 0
   116	    const pulseRatio = dailyBaseline > 0 ? last24h / dailyBaseline : 0
   117	
   118	    const sourceMap = new Map<string, number>()
   119	    const tagMap = new Map<string, number>()
   120	
   121	    for (const item of items) {
   122	      sourceMap.set(item.source, (sourceMap.get(item.source) ?? 0) + 1)
   123	      for (const tag of item.tags ?? []) {
   124	        tagMap.set(tag, (tagMap.get(tag) ?? 0) + 1)
   125	      }
   126	    }
   127	
   128	    const sources = Array.from(sourceMap.entries())
   129	      .map(([source, count]) => ({ source, count }))
   130	      .sort((a, b) => b.count - a.count)
   131	    const tags = Array.from(tagMap.entries())
   132	      .map(([tag, count]) => ({ tag, count }))
   133	      .sort((a, b) => b.count - a.count)
   134	
   135	    return {
   136	      last24h,
   137	      pulseRatio,
   138	      uniqueSources: sourceMap.size,
   139	      uniqueTags: tagMap.size,
   140	      sources,
   141	      tags,
   142	      topSourceCount: sources[0]?.count ?? 0,
   143	      topTagCount: tags[0]?.count ?? 0,
   144	    }
   145	  }, [items, clientNow])
   146	
   147	  return (
   148	    <BackendShell>
   149	      <div className="w-full space-y-8">
   150	        <header className="flex flex-col md:flex-row justify-between items-start md:items-center gap-4">
   151	          <div>
   152	            <div className="flex items-center gap-3 mb-1">
   153	              <Gavel className="w-8 h-8" />
   154	              <h1 className="text-3xl md:text-5xl font-bold tracking-tight text-white">
   155	                Policy Intelligence
   156	              </h1>
   157	            </div>
   158	            <p className="text-slate-400 text-sm font-mono">
   159	              Federal regulations, executive actions, and congressional activity affecting soybean oil procurement
   160	            </p>
   161	          </div>
   162	        </header>
   163	
   164	        {analytics.pulseRatio >= 1.5 && (
   165	          <div className="p-3 rounded-xl border bg-amber-500/5 border-amber-500/20 flex items-center gap-3">
   166	            <div className="relative flex h-2.5 w-2.5 text-amber-400">
   167	              <span className="animate-ping absolute inline-flex h-full w-full rounded-full bg-current opacity-75" />
   168	              <span className="relative inline-flex rounded-full h-2.5 w-2.5 bg-current" />
   169	            </div>
   170	            <Radio size={12} className="text-amber-400" />
   171	            <span className="text-xs font-bold uppercase tracking-wider text-amber-400">
   172	              News Pulse {analytics.pulseRatio.toFixed(1)}x
   173	            </span>
   174	            <span className="text-xs text-slate-500">
   175	              {analytics.last24h} items in 24h
   176	            </span>
   177	          </div>
   178	        )}
   179	
   180	        <div className="grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-5 gap-4">
   181	          <MetricCard
   182	            title="Policy Items"
   183	            value={items.length}
   184	            subtext="Latest merged federal and congressional feed"
   185	            icon={Newspaper}
   186	          />
   187	          <MetricCard
   188	            title="Active Sources"
   189	            value={analytics.uniqueSources}
   190	            subtext="Distinct source systems in current feed"
   191	            icon={Building2}
   192	          />
   193	          <MetricCard
   194	            title="24h Activity"
   195	            value={analytics.last24h}
   196	            subtext="Items published in the last 24 hours"
   197	            icon={Activity}
   198	          />
   199	          <MetricCard
   200	            title="Tagged Signals"
   201	            value={analytics.uniqueTags}
   202	            subtext="Unique policy tags attached to records"
   203	            icon={Tag}
   204	          />
   205	          <MetricCard
   206	            title="Velocity"
   207	            value={analytics.pulseRatio > 0 ? `${analytics.pulseRatio.toFixed(1)}x` : "0x"}
   208	            subtext="24h publication rate vs 7-day baseline"
   209	            icon={Radio}
   210	          />
   211	        </div>
   212	
   213	        <section className="bg-[#0a0a0a] border border-white/10 rounded-2xl p-8 md:p-10 hover:border-white/20 transition-all duration-300">
   214	          <div className="text-sm font-semibold text-white uppercase tracking-widest border-l-2 border-cyan-500 pl-3 mb-8">
   215	            {cards?.feedSummary?.title ?? "Live Policy Feed"}
   216	          </div>
   217	          {cards?.feedSummary?.body && (
   218	            <p className="text-sm text-slate-400 mb-6">{cards.feedSummary.body}</p>
   219	          )}
   220	
   221	          {loading ? (
   222	            <div className="space-y-4">
   223	              {Array.from({ length: 6 }).map((_, i) => (
   224	                <div key={i} className="h-20 rounded-xl bg-white/5 animate-pulse" />
   225	              ))}
   226	            </div>
   227	          ) : items.length === 0 ? (
   228	            <p className="text-slate-500 text-sm">Hard stop: no verified legislation feed rows are available.</p>
   229	          ) : (
   230	            <div className="space-y-3">
   231	              {items.slice(0, 30).map((item, idx) => (
   232	                <div
   233	                  key={`${item.source}-${item.title}-${idx}`}
   234	                  className="bg-black/20 border border-white/10 rounded-xl p-4 md:p-5 flex flex-col md:flex-row md:items-start md:justify-between gap-4 hover:border-white/20 transition-all"
   235	                >
   236	                  <div className="min-w-0">
   237	                    <h3 className="text-white font-semibold mb-2 leading-snug">{item.title}</h3>
   238	                    <div className="flex gap-2 flex-wrap">
   239	                      {(item.tags ?? []).slice(0, 6).map((tag) => (
   240	                        <span
   241	                          key={tag}
   242	                          className="px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-xs bg-slate-800 text-slate-300 border border-white/10"
   243	                        >
   244	                          {tag}
   245	                        </span>
   246	                      ))}
   247	                    </div>
   248	                  </div>
   249	                  <div className="text-right whitespace-nowrap shrink-0">
   250	                    <div className="text-xs text-slate-400 font-mono">{item.source}</div>
   251	                    <div className="text-xs text-slate-500 mt-1">{formatDisplayDate(item.publishedAt)}</div>
   252	                    <div className="text-[10px] text-slate-600 mt-1">
   253	                      {hoursAgo(item.publishedAt, clientNow)}
   254	                    </div>
   255	                  </div>
   256	                </div>
   257	              ))}
   258	            </div>
   259	          )}
   260	        </section>
   261	
   262	        <div className="grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-2 gap-6">
   263	          <section className="bg-[#0a0a0a] border border-white/10 rounded-2xl p-8 hover:border-white/20 transition-all duration-300">
   264	            <div className="text-sm font-semibold text-white uppercase tracking-widest border-l-2 border-emerald-500 pl-3 mb-6">
   265	              {cards?.sourcePressure?.title ?? "Source Activity"}
   266	            </div>
   267	            {cards?.sourcePressure?.body && (
   268	              <p className="text-sm text-slate-400 mb-6">{cards.sourcePressure.body}</p>
   269	            )}
   270	            {analytics.sources.length === 0 ? (
   271	              <p className="text-slate-500 text-sm">Hard stop: no verified source-activity rows are available.</p>
   272	            ) : (
   273	              <div className="space-y-4">
   274	                {analytics.sources.slice(0, 10).map((source) => (
   275	                  <div key={source.source}>
   276	                    <div className="flex justify-between text-sm mb-1">
   277	                      <span className="text-slate-300 truncate pr-3">{source.source}</span>
   278	                      <span className="text-slate-400 font-mono">{source.count}</span>
   279	                    </div>
   280	                    <div className="h-2.5 bg-slate-800 rounded-full overflow-hidden">
