     1	<a href="https://demo-nextjs-with-supabase.vercel.app/">
     2	  <img alt="Next.js and Supabase Starter Kit - the fastest way to build apps with Next.js and Supabase" src="https://demo-nextjs-with-supabase.vercel.app/opengraph-image.png">
     3	  <h1 align="center">Next.js and Supabase Starter Kit</h1>
     4	</a>
     5	
     6	<p align="center">
     7	 The fastest way to build apps with Next.js and Supabase
     8	</p>
     9	
    10	<p align="center">
    11	  <a href="#features"><strong>Features</strong></a> ·
    12	  <a href="#demo"><strong>Demo</strong></a> ·
    13	  <a href="#deploy-to-vercel"><strong>Deploy to Vercel</strong></a> ·
    14	  <a href="#clone-and-run-locally"><strong>Clone and run locally</strong></a> ·
    15	  <a href="#feedback-and-issues"><strong>Feedback and issues</strong></a>
    16	  <a href="#more-supabase-examples"><strong>More Examples</strong></a>
    17	</p>
    18	<br/>
    19	
    20	## Features
    21	
    22	- Works across the entire [Next.js](https://nextjs.org) stack
    23	  - App Router
    24	  - Pages Router
    25	  - Proxy
    26	  - Client
    27	  - Server
    28	  - It just works!
    29	- supabase-ssr. A package to configure Supabase Auth to use cookies
    30	- Password-based authentication block installed via the [Supabase UI Library](https://supabase.com/ui/docs/nextjs/password-based-auth)
    31	- Styling with [Tailwind CSS](https://tailwindcss.com)
    32	- Components with [shadcn/ui](https://ui.shadcn.com/)
    33	- Optional deployment with [Supabase Vercel Integration and Vercel deploy](#deploy-your-own)
    34	  - Environment variables automatically assigned to Vercel project
    35	
    36	## Demo
    37	
    38	You can view a fully working demo at [demo-nextjs-with-supabase.vercel.app](https://demo-nextjs-with-supabase.vercel.app/).
    39	
    40	## Deploy to Vercel
    41	
    42	Vercel deployment will guide you through creating a Supabase account and project.
    43	
    44	After installation of the Supabase integration, all relevant environment variables will be assigned to the project so the deployment is fully functioning.
    45	
    46	[![Deploy with Vercel](https://vercel.com/button)](https://vercel.com/new/clone?repository-url=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fvercel%2Fnext.js%2Ftree%2Fcanary%2Fexamples%2Fwith-supabase&project-name=nextjs-with-supabase&repository-name=nextjs-with-supabase&demo-title=nextjs-with-supabase&demo-description=This+starter+configures+Supabase+Auth+to+use+cookies%2C+making+the+user%27s+session+available+throughout+the+entire+Next.js+app+-+Client+Components%2C+Server+Components%2C+Route+Handlers%2C+Server+Actions+and+Middleware.&demo-url=https%3A%2F%2Fdemo-nextjs-with-supabase.vercel.app%2F&external-id=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fvercel%2Fnext.js%2Ftree%2Fcanary%2Fexamples%2Fwith-supabase&demo-image=https%3A%2F%2Fdemo-nextjs-with-supabase.vercel.app%2Fopengraph-image.png)
    47	
    48	The above will also clone the Starter kit to your GitHub, you can clone that locally and develop locally.
    49	
    50	If you wish to just develop locally and not deploy to Vercel, [follow the steps below](#clone-and-run-locally).
    51	
    52	## Clone and run locally
    53	
    54	1. You'll first need a Supabase project which can be made [via the Supabase dashboard](https://database.new)
    55	
    56	2. Create a Next.js app using the Supabase Starter template npx command
    57	
    58	   ```bash
    59	   npx create-next-app --example with-supabase with-supabase-app
    60	   ```
    61	
    62	   ```bash
    63	   yarn create next-app --example with-supabase with-supabase-app
    64	   ```
    65	
    66	   ```bash
    67	   pnpm create next-app --example with-supabase with-supabase-app
    68	   ```
    69	
    70	3. Use `cd` to change into the app's directory
    71	
    72	   ```bash
    73	   cd with-supabase-app
    74	   ```
    75	
    76	4. Rename `.env.example` to `.env.local` and update the following:
    77	
    78	  ```env
    79	  NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=[INSERT SUPABASE PROJECT URL]
    80	  NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=[INSERT SUPABASE PROJECT API PUBLISHABLE OR ANON KEY]
    81	  ```
    82	  > [!NOTE]
    83	  > This example uses `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY`, which refers to Supabase's new **publishable** key format.
    84	  > Both legacy **anon** keys and new **publishable** keys can be used with this variable name during the transition period. Supabase's dashboard may show `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`; its value can be used in this example.
    85	  > See the [full announcement](https://github.com/orgs/supabase/discussions/29260) for more information.
    86	
    87	  Both `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY` can be found in [your Supabase project's API settings](https://supabase.com/dashboard/project/_?showConnect=true)
    88	
    89	5. You can now run the Next.js local development server:
    90	
    91	   ```bash
    92	   npm run dev
    93	   ```
    94	
    95	   The starter kit should now be running on [localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000/).
    96

    98

I’m generating `quality/exploration_role_map.json` now from `git ls-files` with disallowed-path filtering and per-file role tagging/rationales, including explicit `skill-tool` citations where applicable.
exec
/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import os, json, subprocess, datetime, re
from pathlib import Path

root = Path('.')
files = subprocess.check_output(['git','ls-files'], text=True).splitlines()

DISALLOWED_PREFIXES = (
    '.git/', '.venv/', 'venv/', 'node_modules/', '__pycache__/',
    '.pytest_cache/', '.mypy_cache/', '.ruff_cache/', '.tox/'
)

def is_disallowed(path: str) -> bool:
    if any(path.startswith(p) for p in DISALLOWED_PREFIXES):
        return True
    parts = path.split('/')
    for part in parts:
        if part.endswith('.egg-info') or part.endswith('.dist-info'):
            return True
    return False

files = [p for p in files if not is_disallowed(p)]

skill_tool_refs = {
    '.github/skills/data-review/scripts/freshness_report.sql': '.github/skills/data-review/SKILL.md:13',
    '.kilo/skills/data-review/scripts/freshness_report.sql': '.kilo/skills/data-review/SKILL.md:13',
}

config_names = {
    '.gitignore', '.vercelignore', 'components.json', 'package.json', 'package-lock.json',
    'next.config.ts', 'postcss.config.mjs', 'tailwind.config.ts', 'tsconfig.json',
    'python/pyproject.toml', '.kilo/kilo.json', 'eslint.config.mjs', '.env.example',
}

skill_prose_exact = {'AGENTS.md','CLAUDE.md','.github/copilot-instructions.md'}

def read_preview(path: Path) -> str:
    try:
        with path.open('r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            for _ in range(20):
                line = f.readline()
                if not line:
                    break
                s = line.strip()
                if s:
                    return s[:160]
    except Exception:
        pass
    return ''


def role_for(path: str) -> tuple[str,str,dict]:
    extra = {}
    p = Path(path)
    name = p.name

    if path.startswith('quality/') or name == 'quality_gate.py':
        return 'playbook-output', 'Generated quality-playbook output surface rather than intrinsic project behavior.', extra

    if path in skill_tool_refs:
        extra['skill_prose_reference'] = skill_tool_refs[path]
        return 'skill-tool', 'Script is explicitly referenced by skill prose for operator execution during the workflow.', extra

    if path.endswith('/SKILL.md') or name == 'SKILL.md':
        return 'skill-prose', 'Declarative skill workflow content with procedural instructions for agent behavior.', extra

    if path.startswith('.github/agents/'):
        return 'skill-prose', 'Agent instruction file defining behavior and workflow constraints.', extra

    if path in skill_prose_exact:
        return 'skill-prose', 'Top-level agent instruction content governing session behavior and constraints.', extra

    if '/references/' in path and (path.startswith('.kilo/skills/') or path.startswith('.github/skills/')):
        return 'skill-reference', 'Reference material consumed by nearby skill prose to drive structured analysis.', extra

    if path.startswith('.kilo/commands/') or path.startswith('.claude/commands/'):
        return 'skill-prose', 'Command prompt content used to invoke structured audit/planning behaviors.', extra

    if path.startswith('.kilo/rules/') or path.startswith('.kilo/workflows/'):
        return 'skill-prose', 'Rule/workflow prose that constrains agent decisions and execution flow.', extra

    if path.startswith('.kilo/skills/') or path.startswith('.github/skills/'):
        if path.endswith('.md'):
            return 'skill-prose', 'Skill-related prose content under skill package directories.', extra

    if path in config_names or path.endswith('.toml') or path.endswith('.json') and path.startswith('.kilo/'):
        return 'config', 'Project/tool configuration file controlling environment, build, or automation behavior.', extra

    if path.startswith('docs/') or path == 'README.md':
        return 'docs', 'Repository documentation describing architecture, plans, operations, or contracts.', extra

    if path.endswith('.md'):
        return 'docs', 'Documentation prose file describing process, design, or operational context.', extra

    if '/tests/' in path or name.startswith('test_') or name.endswith('_test.py') or name.endswith('.test.ts') or name.endswith('.test.tsx'):
        return 'test', 'Automated test case or test harness used to validate behavior contracts.', extra

    if path.startswith('scripts/verify/') or path.startswith('scripts/validate/'):
        return 'test', 'Verification/parity harness script used for gate-style behavioral checks.', extra

    if path.startswith('app/config/') and path.endswith('.json'):
        return 'config', 'Runtime AI snapshot/config payload consumed by API routes.', extra

    if path.endswith(('.ico','.png','.jpg','.jpeg','.svg','.glb')):
        return 'code', 'Runtime static asset served by the application UI.', extra

    if path.endswith(('.ts','.tsx','.js','.mjs','.cjs','.py','.sql','.sh','.pine','.d.ts')):
        return 'code', 'Executable application/library/migration/runtime source file.', extra

    return 'code', 'Tracked runtime artifact participating in repository behavior.', extra

out_files = []
for path in files:
    full = root / path
    role, base_rat, extra = role_for(path)
    size = full.stat().st_size if full.exists() else 0
    preview = read_preview(full)

    if role == 'skill-prose' and preview:
        rationale = f\"{base_rat} Evidence: starts with '{preview}'.\"
    elif role == 'skill-reference' and preview:
        rationale = f\"{base_rat} Evidence: reference heading/prose begins '{preview}'.\"
    elif role == 'docs' and preview:
        rationale = f\"{base_rat} Evidence: document content begins '{preview}'.\"
    elif role == 'config' and preview:
        rationale = f\"{base_rat} Evidence: config declaration begins '{preview}'.\"
    elif role == 'test' and preview:
        rationale = f\"{base_rat} Evidence: test/verification content begins '{preview}'.\"
    elif role == 'skill-tool':
        rationale = base_rat
    else:
        rationale = base_rat

    entry = {
        'path': path,
        'role': role,
        'size_bytes': int(size),
        'rationale': rationale,
    }
    entry.update(extra)
    out_files.append(entry)

if len(out_files) > 2000:
    raise SystemExit(f\"role map too large: {len(out_files)}\")

payload = {
    'schema_version': '1.0',
    'timestamp_start': datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).isoformat().replace('+00:00','Z'),
    'provenance': 'git-ls-files',
    'files': out_files,
}

qdir = Path('quality')
qdir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
(Path('quality/exploration_role_map.json')).write_text(json.dumps(payload, indent=2, ensure_ascii=True), encoding='utf-8')
print(f\"wrote quality/exploration_role_map.json with {len(out_files)} entries\")
