   530	export async function GET() {
   531	  try {
   532	    const supabase = createSupabaseAdminClient();
   533	    const aiSnapshot = await readAiRiskFactorsSnapshot();
   534	    const responseGeneratedAt = new Date().toISOString();
   535	
   536	    const [{ data: metricRows, error: metricError }, { data: attributionRows, error: attributionError }] = await Promise.all([
   537	      supabase
   538	        .schema("analytics")
   539	        .from("dashboard_metrics")
   540	        .select("trade_date, metric_key, metric_value")
   541	        .order("trade_date", { ascending: false })
   542	        .limit(500),
   543	      supabase
   544	        .schema("analytics")
   545	        .from("driver_attribution_1d")
   546	        .select("trade_date, rank, factor, contribution, confidence")
   547	        .order("trade_date", { ascending: false })
   548	        .order("rank", { ascending: true })
   549	        .limit(100),
   550	    ]);
   551	
   552	    if (metricError || attributionError) {
   553	      return NextResponse.json(
   554	        {
   555	          error: metricError?.message ?? attributionError?.message ?? "Failed to load market drivers",
   556	        },
   557	        { status: 500 },
   558	      );
   559	    }
   560	
   561	    const latestMetricDate = metricRows?.[0]?.trade_date ?? null;
   562	    const latestMetrics = (metricRows ?? []).filter((r) => r.trade_date === latestMetricDate);
   563	    const metricMap = new Map<string, number | null>();
   564	    for (const row of latestMetrics) {
   565	      metricMap.set(normalizeMetricKey(row.metric_key), toNumber(row.metric_value));
   566	    }
   567	
   568	    const latestAttributionDate = attributionRows?.[0]?.trade_date ?? null;
   569	    const latestAttribution = (attributionRows ?? []).filter((r) => r.trade_date === latestAttributionDate);
   570	    const attributionByDriver = new Map<DriverKey, { trade_date: string; factor: string; contribution: number | null }>();
   571	    for (const row of latestAttribution) {
   572	      const driverKey = mapFactorToDriver(String(row.factor));
   573	      if (!driverKey) continue;
   574	      const contribution = toNumber(row.contribution);
   575	      const existing = attributionByDriver.get(driverKey);
   576	      const existingAbs = existing?.contribution === null || existing?.contribution === undefined ? -1 : Math.abs(existing.contribution);
   577	      const currentAbs = contribution === null ? -1 : Math.abs(contribution);
   578	      if (!existing || currentAbs > existingAbs) {
   579	        attributionByDriver.set(driverKey, {
   580	          trade_date: String(row.trade_date),
   581	          factor: String(row.factor),
   582	          contribution,
   583	        });
   584	      }
   585	    }
   586	
   587	    const scoreCandidates = {
   588	      vix_stress: coerceScore(getMetric(metricMap, ["vix_stress_score", "vix_score", "market_volatility_score", "driver_vix_score"])),
   589	      crush_pressure: coerceScore(getMetric(metricMap, ["crush_pressure_score", "crush_score", "driver_crush_score"])),
   590	      china_tension: coerceScore(getMetric(metricMap, ["china_tension_score", "china_score", "driver_china_score"])),
   591	      tariff_threat: coerceScore(getMetric(metricMap, ["tariff_threat_score", "tariff_score", "policy_risk_score", "driver_tariff_score"])),
   592	      energy_stress: coerceScore(getMetric(metricMap, ["energy_stress_score", "energy_score", "driver_energy_score"])),
   593	    };
   594	
   595	    for (const key of Object.keys(scoreCandidates) as DriverKey[]) {
   596	      if (scoreCandidates[key] === null) {
   597	        scoreCandidates[key] = coerceScore(attributionByDriver.get(key)?.contribution ?? null);
   598	      }
   599	    }
   600	
   601	    const normalized = (value: number, floor: number, ceil: number): number =>
   602	      Math.max(0, Math.min(100, ((value - floor) / (ceil - floor)) * 100));
   603	
   604	    if (scoreCandidates.vix_stress === null) {
   605	      const vix = getMetric(metricMap, ["vix_value"]);
   606	      const ovx = getMetric(metricMap, ["ovx_value"]);
   607	      if (vix !== null || ovx !== null) {
   608	        const vixScore = vix === null ? 0 : normalized(vix, 14, 40);
   609	        const ovxScore = ovx === null ? 0 : normalized(ovx, 20, 60);
   610	        scoreCandidates.vix_stress = Math.round((vixScore * 0.55 + ovxScore * 0.45) * 10) / 10;
   611	      }
   612	    }
   613	
   614	    if (scoreCandidates.energy_stress === null) {
   615	      const cl5d = getMetric(metricMap, ["cl_change_5d", "crude_oil_change_5d"]);
   616	      const ovx = getMetric(metricMap, ["ovx_value"]);
   617	      if (cl5d !== null || ovx !== null) {
   618	        const clScore = cl5d === null ? 0 : normalized(Math.abs(cl5d * 100), 0.5, 8);
   619	        const ovxScore = ovx === null ? 0 : normalized(ovx, 20, 60);
   620	        scoreCandidates.energy_stress = Math.round((clScore * 0.5 + ovxScore * 0.5) * 10) / 10;
   621	      }
   622	    }
   623	
   624	    if (scoreCandidates.china_tension === null) {
   625	      const cny = getMetric(metricMap, ["cny_rate"]);
   626	      if (cny !== null) {
   627	        scoreCandidates.china_tension = Math.round(normalized(Math.abs(cny - 7.0), 0.03, 0.35) * 10) / 10;
   628	      }
   629	    }
   630	
   631	    const metricDate = latestMetricDate ? String(latestMetricDate) : null;
   632	    const dateFor = (key: DriverKey): string | null =>
   633	      attributionByDriver.get(key)?.trade_date ?? metricDate;
   634

   721	      key: DriverKey,

   751	      };
   752	
   753	      return {
   754	        ...mergedPayload,
   755	        whatsHappening: ai?.whatsHappening,
   756	      };

    96	function buildProvenance(
    97	  generatedAt: string,
    98	  tradeDate: string | null,
    99	  cardKey: keyof StrategyCards,
   100	  trustedUrls: string[],
   101	): AiCardProvenance {
   102	  return {
   103	    asOf: tradeDate ?? generatedAt,
   104	    generatedAt,
   105	    method: "verified-db-and-trusted-market-pull",
   106	    sourceFeeds: [
   107	      "analytics.market_posture",
   108	      "analytics.dashboard_metrics",
   109	      "analytics.driver_attribution_1d",
   110	      ...trustedUrls,
   111	    ],
   112	    sourceRecords: [
   113	      {
   114	        source: "analytics.market_posture",
   115	        table: "analytics.market_posture",
   116	        recordHint: tradeDate ? `trade_date=${tradeDate}` : "latest row",
   117	        observedAt: tradeDate ?? undefined,
   118	      },
   119	      {
   120	        source: "analytics.driver_attribution_1d",
   121	        table: "analytics.driver_attribution_1d",
   122	        recordHint: `card=${cardKey}`,
   123	        observedAt: tradeDate ?? generatedAt,
   124	      },
   125	    ],
   126	    notes: [
   127	      "Buyer posture is interpreted from verified table and market-source pulls.",
   128	      "Hard stop is applied when required driver or market evidence is missing.",
   129	    ],
   130	  };
   131	}
   132	
   133	export async function GET() {
   134	  try {
   135	    const supabase = createSupabaseAdminClient();
   136	    const aiSnapshot = await readAiSnapshot<StrategyAiSnapshot>(
   137	      "app/config/strategy-posture-ai.json",
   138	    );
   139	    const trustedMarket = await fetchTrustedMarketSnapshot();
   140	    const trustedUrls = uniqueTrustedMarketUrls(trustedMarket);
   141	
   142	    const [{ data: row, error }, { data: attributionRows }, { data: metricRows }] = await Promise.all([
   143	      supabase
   144	        .schema("analytics")
   145	        .from("market_posture")
   146	        .select("posture, rationale, trade_date")
   147	        .order("trade_date", { ascending: false })
   148	        .limit(1)
   149	        .maybeSingle(),
   150	      supabase
   151	        .schema("analytics")
   152	        .from("driver_attribution_1d")
   153	        .select("trade_date, rank, factor, contribution")
   154	        .order("trade_date", { ascending: false })
   155	        .order("rank", { ascending: true })
   156	        .limit(40),
   157	      supabase
   158	        .schema("analytics")
   159	        .from("dashboard_metrics")
   160	        .select("trade_date, metric_key, metric_value")
   161	        .order("trade_date", { ascending: false })
   162	        .limit(300),
   163	    ]);
   164	
   165	    if (error) {
   166	      return NextResponse.json(
   167	        { ok: false, data: null, asOf: new Date().toISOString(), error: error.message },
   168	        { status: 500 },
   169	      );
   170	    }
   171	
   172	    const latestAttrDate = attributionRows?.[0]?.trade_date ?? null;
   173	    const latestAttr = (attributionRows ?? []).filter((r) => r.trade_date === latestAttrDate);
   174	    const topFactors = latestAttr
   175	      .slice(0, 4)
   176	      .map((r) => `${String(r.factor)} (${Number(r.contribution).toFixed(2)})`);
   177	
   178	    const latestMetricDate = metricRows?.[0]?.trade_date ?? null;
   179	    const latestMetrics = (metricRows ?? []).filter((r) => r.trade_date === latestMetricDate);
   180	    const metricMap = new Map<string, number>();
   181	    for (const rowMetric of latestMetrics) {
   182	      const n = Number(rowMetric.metric_value);
   183	      if (Number.isFinite(n)) {
   184	        metricMap.set(String(rowMetric.metric_key).toLowerCase(), n);
   185	      }
   186	    }
   187	
   188	    const dbVix = metricMap.get("vix_value") ?? null;
   189	    const dbOvx = metricMap.get("ovx_value") ?? null;
   190	    const dbCl5d = metricMap.get("cl_change_5d") ?? metricMap.get("crude_oil_change_5d") ?? null;
   191	    const liveVix = trustedMarket.vix.value ?? dbVix;
   192	    const liveOvx = trustedMarket.ovx.value ?? dbOvx;
   193	    const liveCl5d = trustedMarket.cl.change5d ?? dbCl5d;
   194	
   195	    const dbPosture: StrategyPosture | null = row
   196	      ? {
   197	          posture: row.posture as StrategyPosture["posture"],
   198	          rationale: row.rationale ?? "",
   199	          updatedAt: row.trade_date ?? new Date().toISOString(),
   200	        }
   201	      : null;
   202	
   203	    const posture: StrategyPosture | null = aiSnapshot?.posture ?? dbPosture;
   204	    const generatedAt = aiSnapshot?.generatedAt ?? new Date().toISOString();
   205	    const tradeDate = row?.trade_date ?? posture?.updatedAt ?? null;
   206	    const stagedExecutionBias =
   207	      posture?.posture === "ACCUMULATE"
   208	        ? "Staged accumulation remains favored."
   209	        : posture?.posture === "WAIT"
   210	          ? "Hold execution optionality and avoid urgency."
   211	          : "Defer new exposure until risk compression is visible.";
   212	    const volatilityLine =
   213	      liveVix !== null && liveOvx !== null
   214	        ? `VIX ${liveVix.toFixed(2)} and OVX ${liveOvx.toFixed(2)} remain active volatility constraints.`
   215	        : "Hard stop: verified VIX/OVX volatility reads are unavailable.";
   216	    const crudeLine =
   217	      liveCl5d !== null
   218	        ? `CL 5-day change is ${(liveCl5d * 100).toFixed(2)}%, informing near-term pass-through risk.`
   219	        : "Hard stop: verified CL 5-day change is unavailable.";
   220	    const topFactorLine =
   221	      topFactors.length > 0
   222	        ? `Latest verified attribution stack: ${topFactors.join(", ")}.`
   223	        : "Hard stop: no verified attribution rows available in analytics.driver_attribution_1d.";
   224	    const noTrustedFeeds =
   225	      liveVix === null && liveOvx === null && liveCl5d === null;
   226	
   227	    const fallbackCards: StrategyCards = {
   228	      contractImpactCalculator: {
   229	        title: "Contract Impact Calculator",
   230	        body: noTrustedFeeds
   231	          ? "Hard stop: trusted market evidence from Yahoo/FRED is unavailable, so contract-impact guidance is blocked."
   232	          : `${stagedExecutionBias} ${volatilityLine} ${crudeLine}`,
   233	        strategicSpecialInstructions: STRATEGY_INSTRUCTIONS.contractImpactCalculator,
   234	        provenance: buildProvenance(generatedAt, tradeDate, "contractImpactCalculator", trustedUrls),
   235	      },
   236	      factorWaterfall: {
   237	        title: "Factor Waterfall",
   238	        body: topFactorLine,
   239	        strategicSpecialInstructions: STRATEGY_INSTRUCTIONS.factorWaterfall,
   240	        provenance: buildProvenance(generatedAt, tradeDate, "factorWaterfall", trustedUrls),
   241	      },
   242	      riskMetrics: {
   243	        title: "Risk Metrics",
   244	        body: noTrustedFeeds
   245	          ? "Hard stop: trusted volatility and crude metrics are unavailable; buyer risk-math output is blocked until verified feeds recover."
   246	          : `${volatilityLine} ${crudeLine} Decision-latency risk should be prioritized over single-window execution risk when these channels remain elevated.`,
   247	        strategicSpecialInstructions: STRATEGY_INSTRUCTIONS.riskMetrics,
   248	        provenance: buildProvenance(generatedAt, tradeDate, "riskMetrics", trustedUrls),
   249	      },
   250	    };
   251	
   252	    const rawCards = aiSnapshot?.cards ?? fallbackCards;
   253	    const cards: StrategyCards = {
   254	      contractImpactCalculator: {
   255	        ...fallbackCards.contractImpactCalculator,
   256	        ...rawCards.contractImpactCalculator,
   257	        strategicSpecialInstructions:
   258	          rawCards.contractImpactCalculator?.strategicSpecialInstructions ??
   259	          STRATEGY_INSTRUCTIONS.contractImpactCalculator,
   260	        provenance:
   261	          rawCards.contractImpactCalculator?.provenance ??
   262	          buildProvenance(generatedAt, tradeDate, "contractImpactCalculator", trustedUrls),
   263	      },
   264	      factorWaterfall: {
   265	        ...fallbackCards.factorWaterfall,
   266	        ...rawCards.factorWaterfall,
   267	        strategicSpecialInstructions:
   268	          rawCards.factorWaterfall?.strategicSpecialInstructions ??
   269	          STRATEGY_INSTRUCTIONS.factorWaterfall,
   270	        provenance:
   271	          rawCards.factorWaterfall?.provenance ??
   272	          buildProvenance(generatedAt, tradeDate, "factorWaterfall", trustedUrls),
   273	      },
   274	      riskMetrics: {
   275	        ...fallbackCards.riskMetrics,
   276	        ...rawCards.riskMetrics,
   277	        strategicSpecialInstructions:
   278	          rawCards.riskMetrics?.strategicSpecialInstructions ??
   279	          STRATEGY_INSTRUCTIONS.riskMetrics,
   280	        provenance:
   281	          rawCards.riskMetrics?.provenance ??
   282	          buildProvenance(generatedAt, tradeDate, "riskMetrics", trustedUrls),
   283	      },
   284	    };
   285	
   286	    const envelope: ApiEnvelope<StrategyPosture | null> = {
   287	      ok: true,
   288	      data: posture,
   289	      asOf: new Date().toISOString(),
   290	      source: ["analytics.market_posture", ...TRUSTED_MARKET_SOURCE_FEEDS].join(","),
   291	    };
   292	
   293	    return NextResponse.json({
   294	      ...envelope,
   295	      cards,
   296	      ai: toAiEnvelopeMeta(aiSnapshot),
   297	    });
   298	  } catch (err) {
   299	    return NextResponse.json(
   300	      { ok: false, data: null, asOf: new Date().toISOString(), error: String(err) },

exec

   141	
   142	function buildProvenance(
   143	  generatedAt: string,
   144	  updatedAt: string,
   145	  cardKey: string,
   146	  trustedUrls: string[],
   147	): AiCardProvenance {
   148	  return {
   149	    asOf: updatedAt,
   150	    generatedAt,
   151	    method: "verified-db-and-trusted-market-pull",
   152	    sourceFeeds: ["alt.news_events", "mkt.cftc_1w", ...trustedUrls],
   153	    sourceRecords: [
   154	      {
   155	        source: "alt.news_events",
   156	        table: "alt.news_events",
   157	        recordHint: "latest 7-day sentiment rows",
   158	        observedAt: updatedAt,
   159	      },
   160	      {
   161	        source: "mkt.cftc_1w",
   162	        table: "mkt.cftc_1w",
   163	        recordHint: "symbol=ZL latest observation",
   164	        observedAt: updatedAt,
   165	      },
   166	      {
   167	        source: "trusted-market-pull",
   168	        recordHint: `card=${cardKey}`,
   169	        observedAt: generatedAt,
   170	      },
   171	    ],
   172	    notes: [
   173	      "Narrative output is buyer-facing procurement interpretation from verified rows.",
   174	      "Hard stop language is required when market or flow evidence is missing.",
   175	    ],
   176	  };
   177	}

   179	export async function GET() {
   180	  try {
   181	    const supabase = createSupabaseAdminClient();
   182	    const aiSnapshot = await readAiSnapshot<SentimentAiSnapshot>(
   183	      "app/config/sentiment-overview-ai.json",
   184	    );
   185	    const trustedMarket = await fetchTrustedMarketSnapshot();
   186	    const trustedUrls = uniqueTrustedMarketUrls(trustedMarket);
   187	
   188	    const sevenDaysAgo = new Date();
   189	    sevenDaysAgo.setDate(sevenDaysAgo.getDate() - 7);
   190	    const oneDayAgo = new Date();
   191	    oneDayAgo.setDate(oneDayAgo.getDate() - 1);
   192	
   193	    const [{ data: newsRows, error: newsError }, { data: cftcRow }] = await Promise.all([
   194	      supabase
   195	        .schema("alt")
   196	        .from("news_events")
   197	        .select("source, published_at, specialist_tags")
   198	        .gte("published_at", sevenDaysAgo.toISOString())
   199	        .order("published_at", { ascending: false })
   200	        .limit(300),
   201	      supabase
   202	        .schema("mkt")
   203	        .from("cftc_1w")
   204	        .select("observation_date, payload")
   205	        .eq("symbol", "ZL")
   206	        .order("observation_date", { ascending: false })
   207	        .limit(1)
   208	        .maybeSingle(),
   209	    ]);
   210	
   211	    if (newsError) {
   212	      return NextResponse.json(
   213	        { ok: false, data: null, asOf: new Date().toISOString(), error: newsError.message },
   214	        { status: 500 },
   215	      );
   216	    }
   217	
   218	    const rows = newsRows ?? [];
   219	    const headlineCount = rows.length;
   220	    const last24h = rows.filter((row) => new Date(row.published_at).getTime() >= oneDayAgo.getTime()).length;
   221	    const pulseRatio = headlineCount > 0 ? last24h / Math.max(1, headlineCount / 7) : 0;
   222	    const flowState = pulseRatio >= 1.5 ? "elevated" : pulseRatio >= 1 ? "balanced" : "subdued";
   223	
   224	    const sourceSet = new Set<string>();
   225	    const tagCounts = new Map<string, number>();
   226	    for (const row of rows) {
   227	      if (row.source) sourceSet.add(row.source);
   228	      const tags = Array.isArray(row.specialist_tags) ? row.specialist_tags : [];
   229	      for (const tag of tags) {
   230	        const normalized = String(tag).trim();
   231	        if (!normalized) continue;
   232	        tagCounts.set(normalized, (tagCounts.get(normalized) ?? 0) + 1);
   233	      }
   234	    }
   235	    const topTags = Array.from(tagCounts.entries())
   236	      .sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1])
   237	      .slice(0, 3)
   238	      .map(([tag]) => tag);
   239	    const tagPhrase = topTags.length > 0 ? topTags.join(", ") : "no dominant specialist cluster";
   240	
   241	    const cotPayload = cftcRow?.payload as Record<string, unknown> | null;
   242	    const cotBias = (cotPayload?.bias as string) ?? "neutral";
   243	    const cotBiasNorm = cotBias.trim().toLowerCase();
   244	    const cotBiasLabel =
   245	      cotBiasNorm.includes("bull") ? "bullish bias" : cotBiasNorm.includes("bear") ? "bearish bias" : "neutral bias";
   246	    const cotBiasScore = cotBiasNorm.includes("bull") ? 20 : cotBiasNorm.includes("bear") ? -20 : 0;
   247	
   248	    const vix = trustedMarket.vix.value;
   249	    const ovx = trustedMarket.ovx.value;
   250	    const cl5d = trustedMarket.cl.change5d;
   251	    const cl5dText =
   252	      cl5d === null ? "n/a" : `${cl5d * 100 >= 0 ? "+" : ""}${(cl5d * 100).toFixed(2)}%`;
   253	    const macroTrustedMissing = vix === null && ovx === null && cl5d === null;
   254	    const volatilityPenalty =
   255	      (vix !== null && vix >= 25 ? -14 : vix !== null && vix < 18 ? 6 : 0) +
   256	      (ovx !== null && ovx >= 35 ? -10 : ovx !== null && ovx < 26 ? 4 : 0) +
   257	      (cl5d !== null && Math.abs(cl5d) >= 0.04 ? -8 : cl5d !== null && Math.abs(cl5d) <= 0.015 ? 4 : 0);
   258	    const flowPenalty = pulseRatio >= 1.5 ? -12 : pulseRatio < 0.8 ? 5 : 0;
   259	    const sentimentScore = Math.max(-100, Math.min(100, cotBiasScore + volatilityPenalty + flowPenalty));
   260	
   261	    const dbOverview: SentimentOverview = {
   262	      headlineCount: headlineCount ?? 0,
   263	      sentimentScore,
   264	      cotBias,
   265	      updatedAt:
   266	        rows[0]?.published_at ??
   267	        (cftcRow?.observation_date ? `${cftcRow.observation_date}T00:00:00Z` : trustedMarket.fetchedAt),
   268	    };
   269	
   270	    const overview: SentimentOverview = {
   271	      headlineCount: aiSnapshot?.overview?.headlineCount ?? dbOverview.headlineCount,
   272	      sentimentScore: aiSnapshot?.overview?.sentimentScore ?? dbOverview.sentimentScore,
   273	      cotBias: aiSnapshot?.overview?.cotBias ?? dbOverview.cotBias,
   274	      updatedAt: aiSnapshot?.overview?.updatedAt ?? dbOverview.updatedAt,
   275	    };
   276	
   277	    const generatedAt = aiSnapshot?.generatedAt ?? new Date().toISOString();
   278	    const fallbackCards: SentimentCards = {
   279	      narratives: [
   280	        {
   281	          title: "Macro Narrative",
   282	          body: macroTrustedMissing
   283	            ? "Hard stop: trusted Yahoo/FRED market context is unavailable; macro narrative classification is blocked."
   284	            : `Macro pressure is ${sentimentScore <= -25 ? "defensive" : sentimentScore >= 25 ? "supportive" : "mixed"} with ${cotBiasLabel}. VIX ${vix?.toFixed(2) ?? "n/a"}, OVX ${ovx?.toFixed(2) ?? "n/a"}, and CL 5-day ${cl5dText} frame near-term narrative risk.`,
   285	          strategicSpecialInstructions: SENTIMENT_INSTRUCTIONS.macroNarrative,
   286	          provenance: buildProvenance(generatedAt, overview.updatedAt, "macroNarrative", trustedUrls),
   287	        },
   288	        {
   289	          title: "Flow Narrative",
   290	          body: headlineCount === 0
   291	            ? "Hard stop: no verified alt.news_events rows in the last 7 days; flow narrative is blocked."
   292	            : `Flow regime is ${flowState} with ${sourceSet.size} active sources and dominant specialist cluster: ${tagPhrase}. Velocity coherence is ${pulseRatio >= 1.5 ? "high-risk noisy" : pulseRatio >= 1 ? "tradable but mixed" : "low-intensity"} for buyer timing decisions.`,
   293	          strategicSpecialInstructions: SENTIMENT_INSTRUCTIONS.flowNarrative,
   294	          provenance: buildProvenance(generatedAt, overview.updatedAt, "flowNarrative", trustedUrls),
   295	        },
   296	        {
   297	          title: "Procurement Narrative",
   298	          body: macroTrustedMissing || !cftcRow
   299	            ? "Hard stop: missing verified CoT or trusted market context prevents buyer-facing procurement narrative."
   300	            : `Buyer pacing should remain ${sentimentScore <= -25 ? "defensive with tighter reassessment windows" : sentimentScore >= 25 ? "opportunistic staged accumulation" : "balanced with optionality preserved"}. CoT is ${cotBiasLabel} and volatility transmission remains ${vix !== null && vix >= 25 ? "elevated" : "contained"} at current read.`,
   301	          strategicSpecialInstructions: SENTIMENT_INSTRUCTIONS.procurementNarrative,
   302	          provenance: buildProvenance(generatedAt, overview.updatedAt, "procurementNarrative", trustedUrls),
   303	        },
   304	      ],
   305	      positioningFlow: {
   306	        title: "Managed Money Positioning",
   307	        body: !cftcRow
   308	          ? "Hard stop: no verified mkt.cftc_1w observation for ZL is available."
   309	          : `Latest CoT posture is ${cotBiasLabel} (observation ${cftcRow.observation_date}). Use this as directional conviction context, not a standalone execution trigger.`,
   310	        strategicSpecialInstructions: SENTIMENT_INSTRUCTIONS.positioningFlow,

   312	      },

    83	const AG_SOY_TAG_HINTS = new Set(["crush", "biofuel", "palm", "tariff", "china", "energy"]);
    84	
    85	function includesAnyTerm(text: string, terms: readonly string[]): boolean {
    86	  return terms.some((term) => {
    87	    const normalized = term.trim().toLowerCase();
    88	    if (!normalized) return false;
    89	    const escaped = normalized
    90	      .replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, "\\$&")
    91	      .replace(/\s+/g, "\\s+");
    92	    const pattern = new RegExp(`(^|[^a-z0-9])${escaped}([^a-z0-9]|$)`, "i");
    93	    return pattern.test(text);
    94	  });
    95	}
    96	
    97	function normalizeTags(raw: unknown): string[] {
    98	  if (!Array.isArray(raw)) return [];
    99	  return raw
   100	    .map((tag) => String(tag).trim())
   101	    .filter((tag) => tag.length > 0);
   102	}
   103	
   104	function isAgSoyPolicyRelevant(params: {
   105	  title: string;
   106	  summary?: string | null;
   107	  tags?: string[];
   108	}): boolean {
   109	  const textBlob = `${params.title} ${params.summary ?? ""}`.toLowerCase();
   110	  const primaryMatch = includesAnyTerm(textBlob, AG_SOY_PRIMARY_TERMS);
   111	  const contextMatch = includesAnyTerm(textBlob, AG_SOY_CONTEXT_TERMS);
   112	  const policyMatch = includesAnyTerm(textBlob, AG_SOY_POLICY_TERMS);
   113	  const tagSet = new Set((params.tags ?? []).map((tag) => tag.toLowerCase()));
   114	  const tagHint = Array.from(tagSet).some((tag) => AG_SOY_TAG_HINTS.has(tag));
   115	
   116	  if (primaryMatch) return true;
   117	  if (contextMatch && (policyMatch || tagHint)) return true;
   118	  if (tagHint && includesAnyTerm(textBlob, ["usda", "epa", "farm", "agricult", "renewable", "biofuel"])) {
   119	    return true;
   120	  }
   121	  return false;
   122	}
   123	
   124	function dedupeLegislationItems(items: LegislationItem[]): LegislationItem[] {
   125	  const seen = new Set<string>();
   126	  const deduped: LegislationItem[] = [];
   127	  for (const item of items) {
   128	    const key = `${item.source}::${item.title}`;
   129	    if (seen.has(key)) continue;
   130	    seen.add(key);
   131	    deduped.push(item);
   132	  }
   133	  return deduped;
   134	}

   208	function buildProvenance(
   209	  generatedAt: string,
   210	  asOf: string,
   211	  cardKey: keyof LegislationCards,
   212	  trustedUrls: string[],
   213	): AiCardProvenance {
   214	  return {
   215	    asOf,
   216	    generatedAt,
   217	    method: "verified-db-and-trusted-market-pull",
   218	    sourceFeeds: [
   219	      "alt.legislation_1d",
   220	      "alt.executive_actions",
   221	      "alt.congress_bills",
   222	      ...trustedUrls,
   223	    ],
   224	    sourceRecords: [
   225	      {
   226	        source: "alt.legislation_1d",
   227	        table: "alt.legislation_1d",
   228	        recordHint: "latest policy rows",
   229	        observedAt: asOf,
   230	      },
   231	      {
   232	        source: "alt.executive_actions",
   233	        table: "alt.executive_actions",
   234	        recordHint: "latest executive rows",
   235	        observedAt: asOf,
   236	      },
   237	      {
   238	        source: "alt.congress_bills",
   239	        table: "alt.congress_bills",
   240	        recordHint: "latest congressional rows",
   241	        observedAt: asOf,
   242	      },
   243	      {
   244	        source: "trusted-market-pull",
   245	        recordHint: `card=${cardKey}`,
   246	        observedAt: generatedAt,
   247	      },
   248	    ],
   249	  };
   250	}

   252	export async function GET() {
   253	  try {
   254	    const supabase = createSupabaseAdminClient();
   255	    const aiSnapshot = await readAiSnapshot<LegislationAiSnapshot>(
   256	      "app/config/legislation-feed-ai.json",
   257	    );
   258	    const trustedMarket = await fetchTrustedMarketSnapshot();
   259	    const trustedUrls = uniqueTrustedMarketUrls(trustedMarket);
   260	
   261	    const [legRes, execRes, billsRes] = await Promise.all([
   262	      supabase
   263	        .schema("alt")
   264	        .from("legislation_1d")
   265	        .select("title, summary, source, published_at, payload")
   266	        .order("published_at", { ascending: false })
   267	        .limit(20),
   268	      supabase
   269	        .schema("alt")
   270	        .from("executive_actions")
   271	        .select("title, summary, source, published_at, payload")
   272	        .order("published_at", { ascending: false })
   273	        .limit(10),
   274	      supabase
   275	        .schema("alt")
   276	        .from("congress_bills")
   277	        .select("title, summary, source, published_at, payload")
   278	        .order("published_at", { ascending: false })
   279	        .limit(10),
   280	    ]);
   281	
   282	    const allRows = [...(legRes.data ?? []), ...(execRes.data ?? []), ...(billsRes.data ?? [])];
   283	    allRows.sort((a, b) => new Date(b.published_at).getTime() - new Date(a.published_at).getTime());
   284	
   285	    const filteredRows = allRows.filter((row) => {
   286	      const payload = (row.payload as Record<string, unknown> | null) ?? null;
   287	      const tags = normalizeTags(payload?.tags);
   288	      return isAgSoyPolicyRelevant({
   289	        title: String(row.title ?? ""),
   290	        summary: row.summary as string | null | undefined,
   291	        tags,
   292	      });
   293	    });
   294	
   295	    const dbItems = dedupeLegislationItems(filteredRows.slice(0, 30).map((row) => {
   296	      const payload = (row.payload as Record<string, unknown> | null) ?? null;
   297	      return {
   298	        source: row.source,
   299	        title: row.title,
   300	        publishedAt: row.published_at,
   301	        tags: normalizeTags(payload?.tags),
   302	      };
   303	    }));
   304	
   305	    const snapshotItems = dedupeLegislationItems((aiSnapshot?.items ?? []).filter((item) =>
   306	      isAgSoyPolicyRelevant({
   307	        title: item.title,
   308	        summary: "",
   309	        tags: item.tags ?? [],
   310	      }),
   311	    ));
   312	    const items: LegislationItem[] = dbItems.length > 0 ? dbItems : snapshotItems;
   313	    const generatedAt = aiSnapshot?.generatedAt ?? new Date().toISOString();
   314	    const asOf = items[0]?.publishedAt ?? trustedMarket.fetchedAt;
   315	
   316	    const sourceCounts = new Map<string, number>();
   317	    const tagCounts = new Map<string, number>();
   318	    for (const item of dbItems) {
   319	      sourceCounts.set(item.source, (sourceCounts.get(item.source) ?? 0) + 1);
   320	      for (const tag of item.tags) {
   321	        const normalized = String(tag).trim();
   322	        if (!normalized) continue;
   323	        tagCounts.set(normalized, (tagCounts.get(normalized) ?? 0) + 1);
   324	      }
   325	    }
   326	    const sortedSources = Array.from(sourceCounts.entries()).sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1]);
   327	    const sortedTags = Array.from(tagCounts.entries()).sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1]);
   328	    const topSource = sortedSources[0];
   329	    const topTagSet = sortedTags.slice(0, 3).map(([tag]) => tag).join(", ");
   330	    const topSourceShare =
   331	      dbItems.length > 0 && topSource ? (topSource[1] / dbItems.length) * 100 : null;
   332	    const concentrationClass =
   333	      topSourceShare === null
   334	        ? "unknown"
   335	        : topSourceShare >= 60
   336	          ? "high concentration"
   337	          : topSourceShare >= 40
   338	            ? "moderate concentration"
   339	            : "broadly distributed";
   340	    const latestItem = dbItems[0] ?? null;
   341	
   342	    const fallbackCards: LegislationCards = {
   343	      feedSummary: {
   344	        title: "Live Policy Feed",
   345	        body: latestItem
   346	          ? `Most recent verified agriculture/soy policy item: "${latestItem.title}" from ${latestItem.source}. Primary watch themes are ${topTagSet || "not yet tagged"} with procurement impact evaluated on implementation timing and enforcement likelihood.`
   347	          : "Hard stop: no verified agriculture/soy policy rows are available across legislation, executive, or congressional feeds.",
   348	        strategicSpecialInstructions: LEGISLATION_INSTRUCTIONS.feedSummary,
   349	        provenance: buildProvenance(generatedAt, asOf, "feedSummary", trustedUrls),
   350	      },
   351	      sourcePressure: {
   352	        title: "Source Activity",
   353	        body: topSource
   354	          ? `Source structure is ${concentrationClass}; ${topSource[0]} currently leads policy flow coverage at ${topSourceShare?.toFixed(1)}%. Confidence should be discounted when concentration remains high without corroboration breadth.`
   355	          : "Hard stop: source-activity interpretation is blocked because no verified feed records are available.",
   356	        strategicSpecialInstructions: LEGISLATION_INSTRUCTIONS.sourcePressure,
   357	        provenance: buildProvenance(generatedAt, asOf, "sourcePressure", trustedUrls),
   358	      },
   359	      tagPressure: {
   360	        title: "Policy Tag Pressure",

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba lib/server/trusted-market-sources.ts | sed -n '1,340p'" in /Users/zincdigital/mnt/SatechiHub/ZINC-FUSION-V16
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	type YahooChartResult = {
     2	  meta?: {
     3	    regularMarketPrice?: number;
     4	    regularMarketTime?: number;
     5	    previousClose?: number;
     6	  };
     7	  timestamp?: number[];
     8	  indicators?: {
     9	    quote?: Array<{
    10	      close?: Array<number | null>;
    11	    }>;
    12	  };
    13	};
    14	
    15	type YahooChartResponse = {
    16	  chart?: {
    17	    result?: YahooChartResult[];
    18	  };
    19	};
    20	
    21	type TrustedSeriesPoint = {
    22	  value: number | null;
    23	  asOf: string | null;
    24	  source: string;
    25	  url: string;
    26	};
    27	
    28	export type TrustedMarketSnapshot = {
    29	  fetchedAt: string;
    30	  zl: TrustedSeriesPoint & { change5d: number | null };
    31	  cl: TrustedSeriesPoint & { change5d: number | null };
    32	  cny: TrustedSeriesPoint & { change5d: number | null };
    33	  vix: TrustedSeriesPoint;
    34	  ovx: TrustedSeriesPoint;
    35	};
    36	
    37	export const TRUSTED_MARKET_SOURCE_FEEDS = [
    38	  "https://query2.finance.yahoo.com/v8/finance/chart/ZL=F?interval=1d&range=1mo",
    39	  "https://query2.finance.yahoo.com/v8/finance/chart/CL=F?interval=1d&range=1mo",
    40	  "https://query2.finance.yahoo.com/v8/finance/chart/CNY=X?interval=1d&range=1mo",
    41	  "https://fred.stlouisfed.org/graph/fredgraph.csv?id=VIXCLS",
    42	  "https://fred.stlouisfed.org/graph/fredgraph.csv?id=OVXCLS",
    43	] as const;
    44	
    45	function toDateIso(ts: number | null | undefined): string | null {
    46	  if (typeof ts !== "number" || !Number.isFinite(ts)) return null;
    47	  return new Date(ts * 1000).toISOString();
    48	}
    49	
    50	async function fetchJson<T>(url: string): Promise<T | null> {
    51	  try {
    52	    const res = await fetch(url, {
    53	      headers: {
    54	        "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Codex Trusted Source Pull)",
    55	      },
    56	      cache: "no-store",
    57	    });
    58	    if (!res.ok) return null;
    59	    return (await res.json()) as T;
    60	  } catch {
    61	    return null;
    62	  }
    63	}
    64	
    65	async function fetchYahooSeries(symbol: string): Promise<TrustedSeriesPoint & { change5d: number | null }> {
    66	  const url = `https://query2.finance.yahoo.com/v8/finance/chart/${encodeURIComponent(
    67	    symbol,
    68	  )}?interval=1d&range=1mo`;
    69	  const payload = await fetchJson<YahooChartResponse>(url);
    70	  const result = payload?.chart?.result?.[0];
    71	  const timestamps = result?.timestamp ?? [];
    72	  const closes = result?.indicators?.quote?.[0]?.close ?? [];
    73	
    74	  let latestClose: number | null = null;
    75	  let latestTs: number | null = null;
    76	  for (let i = closes.length - 1; i >= 0; i -= 1) {
    77	    const close = closes[i];
    78	    if (typeof close === "number" && Number.isFinite(close)) {
    79	      latestClose = close;
    80	      latestTs = timestamps[i] ?? null;
    81	      break;
    82	    }
    83	  }
    84	
    85	  let anchorClose: number | null = null;
    86	  let validSeen = 0;
    87	  for (let i = closes.length - 1; i >= 0; i -= 1) {
    88	    const close = closes[i];
    89	    if (typeof close !== "number" || !Number.isFinite(close)) continue;
    90	    validSeen += 1;
    91	    if (validSeen >= 6) {
    92	      anchorClose = close;
    93	      break;
    94	    }
    95	  }
    96	
    97	  const change5d =
    98	    latestClose !== null && anchorClose !== null && anchorClose !== 0
    99	      ? (latestClose - anchorClose) / anchorClose
   100	      : null;
   101	
   102	  return {
   103	    value: latestClose,
   104	    asOf: toDateIso(latestTs ?? result?.meta?.regularMarketTime ?? null),
   105	    source: "Yahoo Finance",
   106	    url,
   107	    change5d,
   108	  };
   109	}
   110	
   111	async function fetchFredLatest(seriesId: string): Promise<TrustedSeriesPoint> {
   112	  const url = `https://fred.stlouisfed.org/graph/fredgraph.csv?id=${encodeURIComponent(seriesId)}`;
   113	  try {
   114	    const res = await fetch(url, { cache: "no-store" });
   115	    if (!res.ok) {
   116	      return { value: null, asOf: null, source: "FRED", url };
   117	    }
   118	    const csv = await res.text();
   119	    const lines = csv.trim().split("\n");
   120	    for (let i = lines.length - 1; i >= 1; i -= 1) {
   121	      const row = lines[i];
   122	      const [date, value] = row.split(",", 2);
   123	      if (!date || !value || value === ".") continue;
   124	      const n = Number(value);
   125	      if (!Number.isFinite(n)) continue;
   126	      return {
   127	        value: n,
   128	        asOf: new Date(`${date}T00:00:00Z`).toISOString(),
   129	        source: "FRED",
   130	        url,
   131	      };
   132	    }
   133	  } catch {
   134	    return { value: null, asOf: null, source: "FRED", url };
   135	  }
   136	  return { value: null, asOf: null, source: "FRED", url };
   137	}
   138	
   139	export async function fetchTrustedMarketSnapshot(): Promise<TrustedMarketSnapshot> {
   140	  const [zl, cl, cny, vix, ovx] = await Promise.all([
   141	    fetchYahooSeries("ZL=F"),
   142	    fetchYahooSeries("CL=F"),
   143	    fetchYahooSeries("CNY=X"),
   144	    fetchFredLatest("VIXCLS"),
   145	    fetchFredLatest("OVXCLS"),
   146	  ]);
   147	
   148	  return {
   149	    fetchedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
   150	    zl,
   151	    cl,
   152	    cny,
   153	    vix,
   154	    ovx,
   155	  };
   156	}
   157	
   158	export function uniqueTrustedMarketUrls(snapshot: TrustedMarketSnapshot): string[] {
   159	  return [snapshot.zl.url, snapshot.cl.url, snapshot.cny.url, snapshot.vix.url, snapshot.ovx.url]
   160	    .filter((u, idx, arr) => typeof u === "string" && arr.indexOf(u) === idx);
   161	}

     1	# AGENTS.md — ZINC-FUSION-V16
     2	
     3	## What This Project Is
     4	
     5	Commodity procurement forecasting system for ZL (soybean oil futures). Clean-room rebuild of legacy baseline on Supabase — **no code transferred, everything written from scratch.**
     6	
     7	**Client:** US Oil Solutions (Las Vegas)
     8	
     9	- **Chris** (owner) BUYS raw soybean oil by the trainload for 100+ restaurant kitchens (Caesars, Boyd, Resorts World)
    10	- **Kevin** (sales director) uses Vegas Intel to pitch restaurants and schedule service around events
    11	- Chris is a BUYER. ZL going UP = bad for his costs. Strategy language (ACCUMULATE/WAIT) reflects this.
    12	
    13	**The migration plan is your bible:** [`docs/plans/2026-03-17-v16-migration-plan.md`](docs/plans/2026-03-17-v16-migration-plan.md) — 1,235 lines, 14 sections, every table/route/job/phase defined.
    14	
    15	---
    16	
    17	## Tech Stack
    18	
    19	| Layer                  | Technology                                              |
    20	| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
    21	| **Database**           | Supabase Postgres — cloud only, no local Supabase       |
    22	| **Schema mgmt**        | Supabase CLI migrations (SQL-first, `db push` to cloud) |
    23	| **Frontend**           | Next.js 14+ on Vercel (frontend hosting ONLY)           |
    24	| **UI System**          | shadcn/ui + Radix primitives + Tailwind CSS             |
    25	| **Data ingestion**     | pg_cron + `http` extension (inside Postgres, $0 cost)   |
    26	| **DB client (TS)**     | Supabase JS client (reads only)                         |
    27	| **DB client (Python)** | psycopg2 to cloud Supabase Postgres; local PostgreSQL only for AG training source |
    28	| **ML**                 | AutoGluon (CPU-only), custom specialist models          |
    29	| **Auth**               | Supabase Auth                                           |
    30	| **API secrets**        | Supabase Vault (accessed via `current_setting()`)       |
    31	| **Package mgr**        | npm (frontend), uv (Python)                             |
    32	| **Env mgmt**           | Vercel <> Supabase integration, `vercel env pull`       |
    33	
    34	---
    35	
    36	## Hard Rules
    37	
    38	### Architecture Rules
    39	
    40	1. **11 specialists — NEVER say 10.** The Big-11: crush, china, fx, fed, tariff, energy, biofuel, palm, volatility, substitutes, trump_effect.
    41	2. **Target = future PRICE LEVEL** (`close.shift(-horizon)`), columns named `target_price_{h}d`. Never returns.
    42	3. **Target Zones = horizontal lines** at price levels. NEVER say: cones, bands, funnels, confidence intervals.
    43	4. **Probability language:** "ZL has an X% chance of hitting XX.XX by [date]" — derived from Monte Carlo (10k runs) + pinball loss + MAE/accuracy %.
    44	5. **No Inngest. No Vercel Cron.** All scheduling via Supabase pg_cron + `http` extension. Vercel is frontend hosting ONLY.
    45	6. **9 schemas:** mkt, econ, alt, supply, training, forecasts, analytics, ops, vegas. No others.
    46	7. **ProFarmer is mandatory** ($500/month). Rebuilt as Python Playwright scraper, not Node.js Puppeteer.
    47	8. **Training gate:** NEVER start model training without explicit user approval.
    48	9. **Chart is sacred.** REWRITE from scratch using legacy baseline as visual reference — zero modifications to behavior. NEVER copy legacy baseline code.
    49	10. **Landing page is sacred.** REWRITE from scratch using legacy baseline as visual reference — preserve the design identity. NEVER copy legacy baseline code.
    50	11. **ZERO mock data.** No placeholders, no temps, no demo/synthetic/random data anywhere, ever. Empty state until real data flows. This is the HARDEST rule.
    51	12. **ZERO code copying.** Every line of V16 is written fresh. legacy baseline is a visual reference only. Clone-and-clean failed catastrophically — never again.
    52	13. **Cloud Supabase only.** Cloud Supabase is canonical. Supabase CLI is for cloud migrations (`db push`) only. No `supabase status`. No `supabase start`. Local PostgreSQL is permitted only as the AG training-source staging database, never as canonical warehouse or local Supabase replacement.
    53	14. **No hardcoded port 3000.** Dev server port must be checked for availability first.
    54	15. **Design holdoff exception (locked 2026-05-07).** For page parity work, do not redesign. Reproduce locked source visuals exactly.
    55	16. **Locked page authority map (2026-05-07):** Strategy=V16, Vegas Intel=V16, Dashboard=V15, Legislation=V15, Sentiment=V15.
    56	17. **Global layout lock:** Full-width geometry is mandatory on ALL V16 pages. Do not keep narrow/containerized widths.
    57	18. **Pixel parity lock:** Typography, spacing, shadows, border/radius, gradients, hex colors, and all interaction states must match locked source exactly.
    58	19. **Cadence pivot lock:** Most non-price data moves to weekly batch ingest and weekly retraining; hourly chart freshness remains.
    59	20. **GPT content lock:** Sentiment and Legislation pipelines are GPT-driven from approved source feeds with traceable provenance.
    60	21. **AI-first cards lock (2026-05-08):** Dashboard/strategy/sentiment/legislation/vegas cards are primarily AI snapshot driven; avoid request-time external data pulls in API handlers.
    61	22. **Weekly pull lock (2026-05-08):** Non-price card refresh jobs run weekly cadence by default; AG training runs by manual batch trigger only unless explicitly changed.
    62	23. **Source reduction lock (2026-05-08):** Keep only strict high-impact feeds (FOMC/FED, ProFarmer, FRED aggregate series, constrained weather stations, core energy/FX/volatility signals).
    63	24. **Symbol budget lock (2026-05-08):** Maintain a compact universe (target ~20-30 symbols); do not reopen 50+ symbol spread without explicit approval.
    64	
    65	### Process Rules
    66	
    67	1. **Read the migration plan before touching code.** It defines every table, route, job, and phase.
    68	2. **Follow the phase order.** Phase 0 before Phase 1. Phase 1 before Phase 2. No skipping.
    69	3. **Run evaluation gates.** Each phase has specific checks. Don't declare done until they pass.
    70	4. **One task at a time.** Finish what was asked before touching anything else.
    71	5. **No "while I'm here" refactors.** Stay scoped.
    72	6. **Memory first.** Search memory at session start, store decisions immediately.
    73	7. **Brainstorm before building.** Use the superpowers brainstorming skill for any non-trivial feature.
    74	8. **Verify before claiming done.** Use the superpowers verification skill.
    75	9. **Checkpoint before implementation.** For non-trivial planning work, audit repo reality first, structure the plan as numbered decision checkpoints, run one Ralph Loop per checkpoint, and update docs after each locked decision.
    76	
    77	### Security Rules
    78	
    79	1. No `service_role` key exposed to browser — ever.
    80	2. `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` vars contain only anon key and URL.
    81	3. No manual `.env` copying. Use `vercel env pull` exclusively.
    82	4. ProFarmer credentials stay local — never deployed to Vercel.
    83	5. RLS enabled on every table from day one.
    84	6. API keys for data ingestion stored in Supabase Vault — not env vars, not hardcoded.
    85	
    86	---
    87	
    88	## Banned Words
    89	
    90	Never use these in code, comments, UI, or conversation:
    91	
    92	- "cones" / "probability cone"
    93	- "confidence band"
    94	- "funnel"
    95	- "cents/lb" (use "ZL futures contract price" or "price")
    96	- "10 specialists" (there are 11)
    97

   100	## Execution Phases (Summary)
   101	
   102	Full details in the migration plan. Quick reference:
   103	
   104	| Phase   | What                            | Key Deliverable                                                                                     |
   105	| ------- | ------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
   106	| **0**   | Infrastructure foundation       | Supabase cloud + Vercel + shadcn/ui + health route                                                  |
   107	| **1**   | Schema & seed                   | All 9 schemas, RLS, indexes, Gate 2 passes                                                          |
   108	| **1.5** | **All page rewrites**           | All 6 pages rewritten from scratch (legacy baseline visual ref only). Empty state until data wired. |
   109	| **2**   | Read path — chart & live price  | Chart renders with real data from Supabase                                                          |
   110	| **3**   | Landing page completion         | Faithful rewrite of legacy baseline landing design                                                  |
   111	| **4**   | Data ingestion (pg_cron+http)   | ZL daily, intraday, FRED, futures — all via Supabase pg_cron                                        |
   112	| **5**   | Python pipeline rebuild         | Full ML pipeline, local files for intermediates, promote to cloud                                   |
   113	| **6**   | Remaining ingestion + ProFarmer | All data sources feeding via pg_cron+http, ProFarmer Playwright                                     |
   114	| **7**   | Dashboard completion            | Target Zones, drivers, regime, cards — all live                                                     |
   115	| **8**   | Secondary pages wiring          | Sentiment, Legislation, Strategy, Vegas Intel — real data                                           |
   116	| **9**   | Auth & observability            | Supabase Auth, monitoring, Gate 3 passes                                                            |
   117	| **10**  | Parallel validation & cutover   | legacy baseline/V16 parity confirmed, traffic switched                                              |
   118	
   119	---
   120	
   121	## Key Supabase Patterns
   122	
   123	### Connection Strategy
   124	
   125	```
   126	Frontend (reads):      Supabase JS client with anon key + JWT
   127	Data ingestion:        pg_cron + http extension (runs inside Postgres — no external connection)
   128	Python (reads):        psycopg2 pooled connection to cloud (port 6543)
   129	Python (training src): local PostgreSQL staging DB for AG training only
   130	Python (promotes):     psycopg2 direct connection to cloud (port 5432) — validated outputs only
   131	Python (intermediates): local parquet files — never written to any database
   132	```
   133	
   134	### Migration Pattern
   135	
   136	```bash
   137	# Create a new migration
   138	supabase migration new <name>
   139	
   140	# Push directly to cloud (no local Supabase needed)
   141	supabase db push
   142	
   143	# Diff against cloud
   144	supabase db diff --linked
   145	```
   146	
   147	Never do manual DDL on cloud. Migrations are the single source of truth. No `supabase status`, no `supabase start`, and no local Supabase workflow.
   148	
   149	### Data Ingestion Pattern (pg_cron + http extension)
   150	
   151	All data ingestion runs inside Postgres as plpgsql functions:
   152	
   153	1. pg_cron triggers the function on schedule
   154	2. `http_get()` fetches from external API (synchronous, in-transaction)
   155	3. Parse JSON response in plpgsql
   156	4. UPSERT to target table
   157	5. Log to `ops.ingest_run`
   158	6. API keys from Supabase Vault via `current_setting()`
   159	
   160	No Vercel cron routes. No CRON_SECRET. No serverless functions for ingestion.
   161	
   162	### RLS Pattern
   163	
   164	```sql
   165	-- Enable RLS
   166	ALTER TABLE schema.table ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
   167	
   168	-- Authenticated users can read
   169	CREATE POLICY "authenticated_read" ON schema.table
   170	  FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (true);
   171	
   172	-- Only service_role can write
   173	CREATE POLICY "service_role_write" ON schema.table
   174	  FOR ALL TO service_role USING (true);
   175	```
   176	
   177	---
   178	
   179	## Tips From Legacy Baseline Experience
   180	
   181	These are hard-won lessons. Don't repeat them.
   182	
   183	### Data Source Gotchas
   184	
   185	- **FRED daily** only fetches `limit=5` (latest obs). Full history needs `refresh_fred_api.py`.
   186	- **EIA API** has been intermittently down since Mar 2026. Build with graceful fallback.
   187	- **MPOB Palm** needs a valid FAS OpenData API key. The legacy baseline key was wrong (FoodData Central, not FAS).
   188	- **Yahoo Finance v8** downsamples to monthly for large date ranges. Use 1-year windows with `period1/period2`.
   189	- **FAS site** (fas.usda.gov) returns HTTP/2 stream errors. Needs retry logic.
   190	- **UCO/Tallow** prices: no free direct API. Use FRED PPI proxies: `WPU06410132` (Tallow PPI) + `PCU3116133116132` (Rendering PPI).
   191	
   192	### AutoGluon Gotchas
   193	
   194	- `TimeSeriesPredictor.load(path, require_version_match=False)` — version check bug fires even when versions match.
   195	- All covariates are OBSERVED, not KNOWN. This limits Chronos2 effectiveness.
   196	- CPU-only on macOS ARM. Deep learning models (DeepAR, TFT, etc.) are disabled.
   197	- Training the 19-model zoo takes significant time. Always `--dry-run` first.
   198	
   199	### Frontend Gotchas
   200	
   201	- The chart uses `lightweight-charts` (TradingView). Settings are precise — don't modify them.
   202	- `ForecastTargetsPrimitive` is a custom series primitive for drawing horizontal Target Zone lines.
   203	- Pivot labels use format `D(P)`, `D(R1)`, `D(S1)`, `W(P)`, `M(P)`, `Y(P)`.
   204	- The NeuralSphere on the landing page uses Three.js + `head.glb`. Resource-heavy but intentional.
   205	
   206	### Supabase-Specific Tips
   207	
   208	- Use `supabase db diff` to check for schema drift between local and cloud.
   209	- Connection pooler (port 6543) has a statement timeout. Use direct (5432) for long writes.
   210	- `pg_cron` jobs run as the `postgres` role. Make sure your functions/procedures are owned by `postgres`.
   211	- Supabase Auth JWTs expire after 1 hour by default. Configure refresh behavior in the client.
   212	
   213	---
   214	
   215	## Vegas Intel — What Makes It Special
   216	
   217	This page is Kevin's primary sales tool. Key features that must survive:
   218	
   219	1. **Events calendar** — CES, SEMA, March Madness, conventions. Links events to oil demand spikes.
   220	2. **Intel buttons** — AI-powered recommendations for each restaurant account.
   221	3. **AI sales strategy** — Generates personalized pitches using real customer data + real oil consumption volumes.
   222	4. **Customer matching** — API pulls real customer records, matches with event impact predictions.
   223	5. **Fryer tracking** — Equipment lifecycle drives service scheduling recommendations.
   224	
   225	This is not a generic dashboard page. It's a sales intelligence tool that directly drives revenue.

   243	## Mandatory Session Startup
   244	
   245	Before ANY response, code, or analysis:
   246	
   247	1. **Memory search** — Query knowledge graph for prior decisions relevant to the current task.
   248	2. **Read the migration plan** — If you haven't read it this session, read it.
   249	3. **Check what phase you're in** — Don't work on Phase 5 stuff if Phase 2 isn't done.
   250	4. **Plan before acting** — Use sequential thinking for multi-step tasks.
   251	
   252	```
   253	Memory(search) -> Plan -> Execute -> Memory(store) -> Report
   254	```
   255	
   256	Every task follows this sequence. No exceptions.

    29	
    30	## 1. Principles
    31	
    32	### What V16 Is
    33	
    34	- A from-scratch rebuild — **no code transferred from legacy baseline**
    35	- legacy baseline stays live as reference and rollback during the entire build
    36	- legacy baseline is a reference for _what the product does_, not a source of code to port
    37	- Purpose: eliminate all drift, errors, mismatches, legacy baggage, Inngest complexity
    38	
    39	### What V16 Is Not
    40	
    41	- Not a migration-in-place
    42	- Not a refactor of legacy baseline
    43	- Not a copy-paste with cleanup
    44	- Not allowed to inherit: the old Prisma migration chain, copied `.vercel/` state, hand-copied `.env.local` habits, the Inngest estate, dead pages, dead jobs, or unclear contracts
    45	
    46	### Non-Negotiable Rules
    47	
    48	1. No old migration history crosses the boundary
    49	2. Every table must have a reader AND a writer
    50	3. Every page must justify its data dependencies
    51	4. Every job must justify its existence
    52	5. Start from the screen, not the schema
    53	6. Rewrite the chart from scratch using legacy baseline as visual reference — settings were hard-won, preserve behavior exactly
    54	7. Rewrite the landing page from scratch using legacy baseline as visual reference — specific and intentional design to preserve
    55	8. ProFarmer is mandatory ($500/month)
    56	9. 11 specialists — never 10. `trump_effect` is the 11th.
    57	10. Target = future price level. Target Zones = horizontal lines. Never cones, bands, or funnels.
    58	
    59	---
    60	
    61	## 2. Product Surface — What V16 Must Deliver
    62	
    63	### Primary Users & Their Needs
    64	
    65	| User                                                                        | Need                                                 | V16 Surface                                       |
    66	| --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
    67	| **Chris** (owner, US Oil Solutions — BUYS raw soybean oil by the trainload) | Know where ZL price is heading to time purchases     | Dashboard chart + Target Zones + Strategy posture |
    68	| **Chris**                                                                   | Understand what's driving price                      | Top 4 Drivers, Regime state, Specialist signals   |
    69	| **Kevin** (sales director)                                                  | Pitch restaurants, time service visits around events | Vegas Intel page                                  |
    70	| **Both**                                                                    | Policy/legislation impact on soy oil                 | Legislation page                                  |
    71	| **Both**                                                                    | Market sentiment read                                | Sentiment page                                    |
    72	
    73	### V16 Page Surface (6 pages — Quant dropped)
    74	
    75	1. **`/`** — Landing page (rewrite from scratch using legacy baseline as visual reference — ZERO code copied, ZERO mock data)
    76	2. **`/dashboard`** — ZL candlestick chart (rewrite from scratch using legacy baseline as visual reference) + Target Zones + live price + regime + drivers + cards
    77	3. **`/strategy`** — Posture recommendation, contract impact calculator, factor waterfall (keep content, redesign layout)
    78	4. **`/legislation`** — Federal Register / policy tracking (clean rebuild)

    98	
    99	- 34 Prisma migrations
   100	- 104 Inngest functions (replaced by pg_cron + http extension + Python workers)
   101	- Local Inngest dev server and healing scripts
   102	- Duplicate/overlapping data ingestion jobs
   103	- Dead tables (`eia_biodiesel_1w` with 0 rows, `uco_prices_1w` with 0 rows)
   104	- Tables with no reader (orphaned)
   105	- `inngest_receipts` table
   106	- Quant page (dropped)
   107	- The entire `.vercel/` state
   108	- Manual `.env.local` habits
   109	- Over-normalized Vegas tables (17 → 7)
   110	- ProFarmer local orchestration complexity (rebuilt as Python Playwright)

   113	
   114	## 3. Target Architecture
   115	
   116	### Stack
   117	
   118	| Layer                  | legacy baseline (current)       | V16 (target)                                                       |
   119	| ---------------------- | ------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
   120	| **Database**           | Prisma Postgres (cloud)         | Supabase Postgres                                                  |
   121	| **Schema mgmt**        | Prisma + 34 migrations          | Supabase migrations (SQL-first)                                    |
   122	| **Frontend**           | Next.js on Vercel               | Next.js on Vercel (new project) + shadcn/ui + Radix + Tailwind CSS |
   123	| **Scheduling**         | Inngest (104 functions)         | pg_cron + http extension (all ingestion inside Postgres)           |
   124	| **DB client (TS)**     | pg.Pool + Prisma for validation | Supabase JS client + pg.Pool for bulk                              |
   125	| **DB client (Python)** | psycopg2 direct                 | psycopg2 direct to Supabase Postgres                               |
   126	| **ML**                 | AutoGluon (local, CPU)          | AutoGluon (local, CPU) — rebuilt clean                             |
   127	| **Specialists**        | 11 Python signal generators     | 11 Python signal generators — rebuilt clean                        |
   128	| **Auth**               | Custom cookie-based             | Supabase Auth                                                      |
   129	| **Env mgmt**           | Manual .env.local               | Vercel <> Supabase integration, `vercel env pull`                  |
   130	| **API secrets**        | Manual env vars                 | Supabase Vault (`current_setting()`)                               |
   131	| **UI system**          | Mixed CSS + shadcn/ui           | shadcn/ui + Radix + Tailwind CSS + ZINC Fusion brand tokens        |
   132	
   133	### Cloud Supabase ONLY — No Local Supabase
   134	
   135	| Scenario                      | Approach                                                                                                                                                  | Why                                                                                                                                                   |
   136	| ----------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
   137	| **Frontend dev**              | Reads from cloud Supabase                                                                                                                                 | Single source of truth. No local/cloud drift. `vercel env pull` provides connection.                                                                  |
   138	| **Python training/inference** | Reads canonical raw data from cloud Supabase, can materialize the bounded AG training source into local PostgreSQL for training, and writes intermediates to **local parquet files** during compute. Promotes validated outputs back to cloud. | Cloud is canonical. Local PostgreSQL is a training-source staging database only, not Supabase and not serving truth. Intermediates stay local during iteration. Only validated artifacts are promoted back to cloud DB. |
   139	| **Data ingestion**            | Runs **INSIDE Postgres** via pg_cron + http extension                                                                                                     | No Vercel cron routes. No external orchestrator. Ingestion is a database-native operation.                                                            |
   140	| **Supabase CLI**              | Used for migrations ONLY (`supabase db push`, `supabase db diff --linked`)                                                                                | No `supabase status`. No `supabase start`. No local Supabase runtime. Local PostgreSQL, when used, is a separate AG training-source database. |
   141	
   142	**Guard rail:** Create a `training` Postgres role that can only write to `training.*` and `forecasts.*` schemas. The Python pipeline uses this role. Frontend service role is read-only on those schemas.
   143	
   144	### Architecture Diagram
   145	
   146	```
   147	+-----------------------------------------------------------+
   148	|                      BROWSER                               |
   149	|  Next.js App (Vercel) -- 6 pages                          |
   150	|  Supabase JS client for auth + reads                      |
   151	+---------------------------+-------------------------------+
   152	                            |
   153	+---------------------------v-------------------------------+
   154	|                       VERCEL                               |
   155	|  Frontend hosting ONLY + read-only API routes              |
   156	|  No cron routes. No data ingestion.                        |
   157	|  Env: auto-injected via Supabase integration               |
   158	+---------------------------+-------------------------------+
   159	                            |
   160	+---------------------------v-------------------------------+
   161	|                      SUPABASE                              |
   162	|  Single cloud DB (9 schemas: mkt, econ, alt, supply,      |
   163	|    training, forecasts, analytics, ops, vegas)              |
   164	|  pg_cron + http extension (ALL data ingestion ~22 jobs)    |
   165	|  Vault (API keys via current_setting())                    |
   166	|  Auth (user authentication)                                |
   167	|  RLS policies per schema                                   |
   168	+-----------------------------------------------------------+
   169	
   170	+-----------------------------------------------------------+
   171	|              LOCAL MACHINE (compute workspace)              |
   172	|  Python ML Pipeline (rebuilt from scratch)                  |
   173	|  - Reads raw data from cloud Supabase via psycopg2         |
   174	|    (cloud is canonical; local PostgreSQL is AG staging only) |
   175	|  - Writes intermediates to local parquet files              |
   176	|  - promote_to_cloud.py pushes validated outputs to cloud   |
   177	|                                                            |
   178	|  ProFarmer Scraper (Python Playwright, system cron)        |
   179	|  - Writes directly to cloud Supabase                       |
   180	+-----------------------------------------------------------+
   181	```
   182	
   183	### Cloud-Canonical Data Contract
   184	
   185	| Layer                                                             | Canonical Location          | Notes                                                        |
   186	| ----------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
   187	| Raw ingest tables (mkt, econ, alt, supply)                        | **Cloud Supabase**          | pg_cron + http writes directly to cloud                      |
   188	| Serving tables (analytics, forecasts.target_zones)                | **Cloud Supabase**          | Dashboard reads from cloud                                   |
   189	| Published forecasts / Target Zones                                | **Cloud Supabase**          | Pre-computed, served to chart                                |
   190	| Training metadata (model_registry, training_runs)                 | **Cloud Supabase**          | Registry of what was trained and when                        |
   191	| Ops observability (ingest_run, pipeline_alerts)                   | **Cloud Supabase**          | All logging in cloud                                         |
   192	| Wide intermediate artifacts (feature matrix, specialist parquets) | **Local compute workspace** | Parquet files during processing only — not canonical storage |
   193	| Model artifacts (AutoGluon model files)                           | **Local compute workspace** | Large binary artifacts, not in any database                  |
   194	
   195	**Training data storage architecture** (whether wide training artifacts should be retained locally vs promoted to cloud `training.*` tables) is a separate checkpoint decision, still gated. Current posture: cloud Supabase `training.*` tables exist and are the intended destination for `promote_to_cloud.py`. Local parquet files are ephemeral compute intermediates. As of the 2026-05-09 training-prep remediation, local prep artifacts live under `data/fusion/`, model artifacts live under `models/fusion/`, and cloud promotion is isolated behind the explicit `promote_to_cloud.py` approval gate. Promotion strips `target_price_{h}d` labels out of cloud `training.matrix_1d.feature_snapshot`; labels remain local-only training columns.
   196	
   197	### Cron-First Ingestion Contract
   198	
   199	All data ingestion runs inside Supabase Postgres via pg_cron + http extension. No ingestion readiness can be claimed until:
   200	
   201	1. **Functions exist** — plpgsql ingestion functions are deployed via migration
   202	2. **Cron jobs are registered** — `SELECT count(*) FROM cron.job` returns expected schedule count
   203	3. **First successful run** — each function has at least one `status = 'ok'` row in `ops.ingest_run`
   204	4. **Vault keys are stored** — `current_setting('app.<key>')` returns non-null for all configured sources
   205	
   206	**Current state (as of planning):** `cron.job` has 0 entries. Extensions pg_cron, http, pg_net are enabled. No ingestion functions exist. This is an explicit blocker for Gate 4.
   207	
   208	---
   209	
   210	## 4. Schema Design (9 Schemas)
   211	
   212	### Schema Consolidation (12 -> 9)
   213	
   214	| Dropped    | Reason                                     | Absorbed Into |
   215	| ---------- | ------------------------------------------ | ------------- |
   216	| `features` | 3 intermediate tables — training artifacts | `training`    |
   217	| `model`    | 3 tables — part of training lifecycle      | `training`    |
   218	| `pos`      | 1 table (`cftc_1w`) — it's market data     | `mkt`         |
   219	
   220	### Schema: `mkt` (Market Data + Positioning)
   221	
   222	| Table          | Purpose                                | Writer                               | Reader                           | Granularity |
   223	| -------------- | -------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------ | -------------------------------- | ----------- |
   224	| `price_1d`     | ZL daily OHLCV — powers the chart      | pg_cron: zl-daily                    | Dashboard chart, all pages       | Daily       |
   225	| `price_1h`     | ZL hourly bars                         | pg_cron: zl-intraday                 | Intraday chart view              | Hourly      |
   226	| `price_15m`    | ZL 15-min bars                         | pg_cron: zl-intraday                 | Intraday chart zoom              | 15min       |
   227	| `price_1m`     | ZL 1-min bars (90-day retention)       | pg_cron: zl-intraday                 | Fine-grain chart                 | 1min        |
   228	| `latest_price` | Most recent ZL price + timestamp       | pg_cron: zl-intraday, pg_cron rollup | Status bar, live ticker          | Real-time   |
   229	| `futures_1d`   | 84 commodity/index futures daily       | pg_cron: databento-futures           | Specialist features, cross-asset | Daily       |
   230	| `options_1d`   | ZL options chain                       | pg_cron: databento-options           | Vol surface                      | Daily       |
   231	| `fx_1d`        | FX rates                               | pg_cron: fx-daily                    | FX specialist                    | Daily       |
   232	| `etf_1d`       | Sector/commodity ETFs                  | pg_cron: etf-daily                   | Substitutes specialist           | Daily       |
   233	| `vol_surface`  | ZL implied vol surface                 | Derived from options_1d              | Volatility specialist            | Daily       |
   234	| `cftc_1w`      | CFTC positioning (absorbed from `pos`) | pg_cron: cftc-weekly                 | Sentiment page, CoT              | Weekly      |
   235	
   236	### Schema: `econ` (Macro/Economic)
   237	
   238	| Table            | Purpose                                                                | Writer                     | Reader                                  | Granularity    |
   239	| ---------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | --------------------------------------- | -------------- |

     1	from __future__ import annotations
     2	
     3	import argparse
     4	from typing import Callable
     5	
     6	from . import build_matrix
     7	from . import generate_forward_forecasts
     8	from . import generate_specialist_features
     9	from . import generate_specialist_signals
    10	from . import generate_target_zones
    11	from . import promote_to_cloud
    12	from . import run_garch
    13	from . import run_monte_carlo
    14	from . import training_readiness_gate
    15	from . import train_models
    16	
    17	PhaseFn = Callable[..., dict[str, object]]
    18	
    19	PHASE_MAP: dict[str, PhaseFn] = {
    20	    "matrix": build_matrix.run,
    21	    "specialists": generate_specialist_features.run,
    22	    "signals": generate_specialist_signals.run,
    23	    "train-readiness": training_readiness_gate.run,
    24	    "train": train_models.run,
    25	    "forecast": generate_forward_forecasts.run,
    26	    "monte-carlo": run_monte_carlo.run,
    27	    "garch": run_garch.run,
    28	    "target-zones": generate_target_zones.run,
    29	    "promote": promote_to_cloud.run,
    30	}
    31	
    32	PIPELINE_ORDER = [
    33	    "matrix",
    34	    "specialists",
    35	    "signals",
    36	    "train-readiness",
    37	    "train",
    38	    "forecast",
    39	    "garch",
    40	    "monte-carlo",
    41	    "target-zones",
    42	    "promote",
    43	]
    44	
    45	
    46	def run_phase(
    47	    name: str,
    48	    *,
    49	    dry_run: bool,
    50	    approve_training: bool,
    51	    approve_promotion: bool,
    52	) -> dict[str, object]:
    53	    fn = PHASE_MAP[name]
    54	    if name == "train":
    55	        return fn(dry_run=dry_run, approved=approve_training)
    56	    if name == "promote":
    57	        return fn(dry_run=dry_run or not approve_promotion, approved=approve_promotion)
    58	    return fn(dry_run=dry_run)
    59	
    60	
    61	def main() -> None:
    62	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ZINC Fusion v16 pipeline runner")
    63	    parser.add_argument("--all", action="store_true", help="Run all phases in pipeline order")
    64	    parser.add_argument("--phase", choices=PIPELINE_ORDER, help="Run one phase")
    65	    parser.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true", help="Show intent only, no writes")
    66	    parser.add_argument(
    67	        "--approve-training",
    68	        action="store_true",
    69	        help="Explicit training approval gate for non-dry-run train phase",
    70	    )
    71	    parser.add_argument(
    72	        "--approve-promotion",
    73	        action="store_true",
    74	        help="Explicit cloud promotion approval gate for non-dry-run promote phase",
    75	    )
    76	    args = parser.parse_args()
    77	
    78	    selected: list[str]
    79	    if args.all:
    80	        selected = PIPELINE_ORDER
    81	    elif args.phase:
    82	        selected = [args.phase]
    83	    else:
    84	        parser.error("Select --all or --phase")
    85	
    86	    for phase in selected:
    87	        result = run_phase(
    88	            phase,
    89	            dry_run=args.dry_run,
    90	            approve_training=args.approve_training,
    91	            approve_promotion=args.approve_promotion,
    92	        )
    93	        print(result)
    94	
    95	
    96	if __name__ == "__main__":
    97	    main()
